text_en: A local anesthetic will be injected into your skin to numb the area where the pacemaker

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: will be inserted.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: The surgeon will make a small incision beneath your collarbone.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: The pacemaker's leads will be inserted into the subclavian vein, and then threaded

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: toward your heart until they reach the right atrium.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: Your surgeon will view x-ray images on a video screen to position the leads correctly.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: Once the leads have been tested, they will be connected to the pulse generator.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: The pulse generator will then be placed in a small pouch that your surgeon will create

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: under the skin beneath your collarbone.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: Your surgeon will then sew the incision back together.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: While you're in the hospital, your doctor will monitor your heart rate and blood pressure

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: to be certain the pacemaker is working properly.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: Before you leave, he or she will make final adjustments to the pacemaker settings.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: After your procedure, over the counter pain medications such as acetaminophen or ibuprofen

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: should be sufficient to relieve any pain.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: You will not be able to drive for about two weeks.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: Periodically, you will need to follow up with your doctor to test and adjust your new

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
text_en: pacemaker.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
text_en: Your surgeon will perform an abdominal aortic aneurysm repair to prevent the rupture of a bulging

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: Your doctor may recommend you receive a blood transfusion to treat your medical condition

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: or to save your life.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: Your blood carries oxygen and nutrients to all of your tissues and removes carbon dioxide

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: and other waste products from them.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: Blood also helps fight infection and heal wounds.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: Blood consists of a liquid portion and cellular components.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: The liquid called plasma is where nutrients, hormones, clotting factors and other chemicals

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: are dissolved.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: The cellular components are suspended in the plasma.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: They consist of red blood cells which transport oxygen and carbon dioxide.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: White blood cells which fight infection.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: Blood cell and platelets which are small subcells that help the blood to clot.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: Blood cells are made in the bone marrow, a soft tissue housed inside many of your bones.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: A blood transfusion uses donor blood to replenish red blood cells, white blood cells, platelets,

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: clotting factors, plasma or whole blood.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: Some stances that may require a blood transfusion include blood loss due to trauma, heart or

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: other major surgery, organ transplants, bleeding disorders such as hemophilia, severe anemia

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: including sickle cell anemia, treatment for leukemia or other types of cancer, disorders

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: that destroy blood cells or bone marrow or RH incompatibility in newborn babies.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: To begin the transfusion an intravenous needle will be inserted into a vein in your hand

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: or arm.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: A bag containing the donor blood will be hung nearby and the blood will drip slowly through

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: a tube attached to the IV needle, allowing it to run through your vein into your bloodstream.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: Throughout the transfusion your temperature, heart rate, breathing and blood pressure will

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: be monitored.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: After the bag is empty if you require more blood, another bag will be attached to the

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: IV tube.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: When your transfusion is completed, the needle will be removed.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: After your transfusion, your doctor will monitor your vital signs to make sure you're

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: not having an adverse reaction.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
text_en: If necessary, you may be given a diuretic to reduce any swelling or fluid retention.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: If you have an abnormal heart rhythm, your doctor may perform electrical cardioversion,

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: where he or she uses an electrical shock to restore your normal heart beat.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: The heart normally beats in a regular rhythm at 60 to 100 beats per minute.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: This steady and coordinated rhythm allows the heart to effectively pump blood.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: The electrical signals that travel through the heart control the heart's rhythm.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: These electrical signals start in a cluster of cells called the Sinoatrial node or pacemaker.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: The Sinoatrial node or SA node is located in the wall of the right upper chamber of the

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: heart, called the right atrium.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: When the electrical signals reach the heart muscle, they cause it to contract and pump blood.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: An abnormal heart rhythm is called an arrhythmia.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: During atrial fibrillation, the atria quiver because of chaotic electrical signals in the

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: heart, resulting in an abnormally fast or irregular heartbeat.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: Because of the abnormal heartbeat, blood may pool in the atria, which can result in

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: the formation of a blood clot.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: If the blood clot passes into the general circulation, it may cause a stroke.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: Before the procedure, you will be connected to an intravenous line.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: You will be sedated so you will sleep through the procedure.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: EKG electrodes will be placed on your chest to monitor your heart's electrical activity.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: Once you are sedated, special cardioversion pads or paddles will be placed on the skin

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: of your chest or your chest and back.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: These pads or paddles will deliver electrical shock through your chest to your heart.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: The shock may be repeated several times until the heart resumes its normal rhythm.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: After your electrical cardioversion, you will be monitored closely in a recovery room

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: or in the coronary care unit of the hospital.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
text_en: Once the sedation wears off and you are in stable condition, you will be set home.

title: ANH00023 Non-cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (NCPTA)
text_en: Non-cardiac percutaneous transliminal angioplasty. A surgeon will perform a non-cardiac percutaneous

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Your surgeon will perform an aortic valve replacement if your aortic valve is not functioning properly.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood through the body.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: It consists of four chambers. The upper chambers are the right and left atria.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: The lower chambers are the right and left ventricles.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: The right and left atria connect to their respective ventricles.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: The right ventricle connects to the pulmonary artery and the left ventricle to the aorta.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Valves are located at these connections to ensure that blood moves in only one direction.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: The parts of a valve that open and close are flaps of tissue called cusps.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: The aortic valve is located between the left ventricle and the aorta.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: When the heart contracts, the aortic valve opens and blood is pumped into the aorta.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: When the heart relaxes, it closes to prevent blood from leaking back into the left ventricle.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: A normal aortic valve has three smooth cusps that open widely and close tightly.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Your aortic valve may be leaky or narrowed, causing your heart to work harder and disrupt your blood flow.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: A leaky valve does not close all the way, causing blood to leak back into the ventricle between each heartbeat.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: This condition is called aortic regurgitation.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: A narrowed aortic valve does not open all the way, which restricts the amount of blood passing out of the ventricle into the aorta.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: This condition is called aortic stenosis.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: You may have been born with an abnormal aortic valve.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: For example, your aortic valve may only have two cusps instead of three, so it may not open and close properly.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Your aortic valve may have become damaged during your lifetime, a condition called acquired aortic valve disease.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Causes include rheumatic fever, which can be a complication of untreated strep throat.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Andocarditis, which is a bacterial infection of the valve, and sclerosis or hardening of the aortic leaflets.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: After a period of time, a faulty aortic valve can cause the heart muscles to become enlarged and weakened.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Depending on the nature of the valve's defect, the walls of the left ventricle may become abnormally thick or thin,

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: and the problem can eventually affect the right side of the heart as well.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Patients with this condition known as heart failure may experience shortness of breath, chest pain or angina pectoris,

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: dizziness, lightheadedness, and or fainting, swelling of the ankles, feet and legs, and fatigue.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: An aortic valve replacement is performed to restore the valve to normal function.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Before your procedure, an IV line will be started in your arm to provide fluids and medications.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: A catheter will be inserted into your bladder to drain urine.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Aortic valve replacements are done under general anesthesia.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: This means you will be asleep for the duration of the operation,

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: and a tube will be placed through your mouth and into your windpipe to help you breathe during surgery.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Once the anesthesia takes effect, your surgeon will begin by making an incision in the chest.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: The traditional incision is made in the middle of the chest, overlying the breastbone or sternum.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: The surgeon then separates the sternum to expose the pericardium, which is a protective membrane encircling the heart.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Your surgeon will then open the pericardium to expose the heart.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: At this point, your heart will be connected to a heart lung machine.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Tubes are used to rerot your blood into this machine, which takes over the functions of the heart and lungs during the operation.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Once the heart lung machine has taken over, your heart will be temporarily stopped and the blood inside removed.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: This keeps the aortic valve still and allows your surgeon to see it.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Your surgeon will open the aorta to expose the aortic valve, make an incision around the edge of the damaged valve, and remove it.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Your surgeon will then carefully sew the replacement valve into place and close the aorta with stitches.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: At this point, blood will again be allowed to flow into your heart to check the function of the new valve.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: If needed, an electric shock will be given to restart the heart.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Once your heart is beating on its own, it will be detached from the heart lung machine.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Generally, temporary pacemaker wires will be placed on the surface of the heart to ensure the heart maintains a normal rhythm during recovery.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: After surgery, you'll be taken to the intensive care unit where your vital functions will be monitored.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: You will still have a breathing tube in your throat, which will prevent you from talking.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
text_en: Several tubes will be in place to drain blood and fluid from your incision, and your bladder catheter will still be draining your urine.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Your doctor may recommend you get a carotid and dardirectomy if your carotid arteries

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: are narrowed or blocked with plaque.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: In your neck are two major blood vessels called the carotid arteries, which carry oxygen

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: rich blood from your heart to your brain.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: You can feel the pulse of the carotid artery in the side of your neck.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Three deposits called atherosclerotic plaques can build up inside the carotid artery.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: These plaques can narrow the passageway of the artery and interfere with blood flow

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: to the brain.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: This blockage or narrowing is called a stenosis.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: If blood flow to the brain is blocked temporarily, a transient ischemic attack, also known as

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: a TIA, may occur.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: When the blood flow is completely obstructed, as in a carotid artery blockage, a stroke

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: can occur.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Before your procedure, an intravenous line will be started.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: A carotid and dardirectomy is usually done under general anesthesia, which will put you

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: to sleep for the duration of the procedure.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: In this case, a breathing tube will be inserted through your mouth and into your windpipe

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: to help you breathe during the operation.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Sometimes a carotid and dardirectomy is done with local anesthesia.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: If local anesthesia is used, you will remain awake, but your neck will be numbed.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: You will probably also receive some sedation.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: On the side of your neck along the blocked artery, the surgeon will make an incision that

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: may run from just behind the ear to a point above the collarbone.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Your surgeon will find the carotid artery and temporarily clamp off blood flow through

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: it.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Your surgeon will open up the artery and may place a shunt or temporary bypass to keep blood

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: flowing to the brain.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Your surgeon will then remove the plaque within the arterial walls.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: After the blockage has been removed, here she will sew the artery back together, usually

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: placing a patch to widen the artery slightly.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: The patch may be made of part of one of your veins or an artificial material such as

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: dachron.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Finally, your surgeon will close the incision with sutures or staples and cover it with

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: bandages.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: A temporary drain is frequently left in the wound.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: After your operation, you will be taken to a recovery area where the staff will monitor

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: your vital signs and neurologic condition.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: You will be given pain medication as needed and will gradually progress from a liquid to

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: a solid diet.

file: ANH00029_Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism.mp4
title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
text_en: If you are a mobile for an extended period of time because of surgery, pregnancy, illness,

file: ANH00029_Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism.mp4
title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
text_en: or travel, you may be at risk for deep vein thrombosis or DVT.

file: ANH00029_Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism.mp4
title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
text_en: A condition where a blood clot forms in a deep vein in the body, typically the leg.

file: ANH00029_Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism.mp4
title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
text_en: Unlike arteries which rely on the heart to force blood through them, veins transport blood

file: ANH00029_Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism.mp4
title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
text_en: with the help of muscle contractions while you move and walk.

file: ANH00029_Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism.mp4
title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
text_en: Plans also have valves that keep blood from flowing backward and pooling in your arms and legs.

file: ANH00029_Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism.mp4
title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
text_en: When you are a mobile for an extended period, your venous circulation slows down.

file: ANH00029_Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism.mp4
title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
text_en: As a result, clotting factors in the blood may cause the slow moving blood to clump together,

file: ANH00029_Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism.mp4

title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
text_en: In some DVT cases, a blood clot may detach from a deep vein and join the bloodstream.

title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
text_en: If a clot is large or if there are many clots, pulmonary embolism may also cause death.

title: ANH11047 Atrial Fibrillation

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: If you are pregnant, you can take folic acid to help prevent certain birth defects in

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: your baby.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: Folic acid and folate are two forms of the same B vitamin.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: Folate is the form that occurs naturally in foods, while folic acid is the man-made

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: form added to multivitamins and fortified foods.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: Your body needs folic acid to help make new cells, including brain cells, blood cells,

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: and DNA and RNA, the genetic material inside your cells.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: During pregnancy, you need even more folic acid because your baby's body needs it too.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: Folic acid helps your baby's brain and spinal cord develop normally.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: Taking folic acid can significantly reduce the risk of neural tube defects in your developing

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: baby, called an embryo at this stage.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: The neural tube begins as a tiny oval-shaped piece of embryonic tissue, which folds into

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: a tube about 28 days after conception.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: When this tube closes properly, it continues to develop into your baby's brain and spinal

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: cord.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: If the neural tube does not close due to the lack of folic acid in your diet, your baby

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: may have a neural tube defect, such as spina bifida or anencephaly.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: In spina bifida, the neural tube fails to close at the bottom.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: As a result, the parts of the spine that enclose and protect the lower spinal cord do not

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: completely form, allowing the cord to protrude.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: Children with this defect often require surgery and have permanent disabilities, including

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: leg paralysis, developmental delays, and nervous system problems.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: In the condition called anencephaly, the neural tube fails to close at the top, preventing

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: large parts of the brain and skull from developing completely.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: These with this defect are unable to live and usually die before birth or soon after.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: Folic acid may also protect against other birth defects, such as cleft lip and palate,

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: and atrial septal defect, which is when a hole between the right and left upper chambers

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: of the heart, fails to close during development.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: As the neural tube develops so early in pregnancy, often before a woman knows she's pregnant,

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: experts recommend all women who are able to become pregnant take a daily multivitamin containing

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: 400 micrograms of folic acid every day.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: During pregnancy, the need increases to 600 micrograms of folic acid each day.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: Experts recommend you take this multivitamin in addition to the food folate in your diet,

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: as your body does not absorb folate found naturally in food, as easily as the man made folic acid.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: Caregivers will often prescribe even 1,000 micrograms of folic acid daily to assure these

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: minimal levels are covered.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: Even more may be required in some groups of women, including those with previous miscarriages

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: and those women deficient in a folic acid metabolic enzyme.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
text_en: Talk with your caregiver about the optimal dose for you.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: The aorta is the largest artery in your body. Your heart pumps fresh, oxygen-rich blood

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: through your aorta to deliver oxygen and nutrients to all your organs and tissues.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: Your aorta is divided into two main sections, the thoracic aorta and the abdominal aorta.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: The thoracic aorta has four parts, the aortic root, ascending aorta, aortic arch, and descending aorta.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: The wall of your aorta is composed of three layers that give it strength, the intima or inner layer,

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: the media or middle layer, and the adventisha or outer layer.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: The elastic muscular media prevents your blood pressure from rupturing the wall.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: Over time, degenerative disease may cause the media layer to break down, weakening the wall of your aorta.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: Blood pumping against the weakened area may cause it to bulge outward like a balloon.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: When this condition occurs in your chest, it is called a thoracic aortic aneurysm.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: These aneurysms may occur in one of several places, including the aortic root, the ascending aorta, or the descending aorta.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: Most thoracic aneurysms have no symptoms, however, when they reach larger sizes, various symptoms may appear, depending on the severity and location of the aneurysm.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: Symptoms of stable thoracic aneurysms may include shortness of breath, chest, back, or abdominal pain, difficulty swallowing, and horse-ness.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: If the aneurysm ruptures or dissects, more dramatic symptoms will appear, including a ripping sensation in the chest, severe pain in the back between the shoulder blades, dizziness, and difficulty walking and speaking.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: If you have these symptoms, you should seek immediate medical attention, as this condition is life-threatening.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: Blood pumping against weakened aortic walls can lead to another life-threatening condition, called aortic dissection, which may or may not occur within an aneurysm.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: Most commonly, aortic dissection begins with damage to the intima layer.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: Blood moves through the break in the intima, separating it from the media, causing bleeding inside the wall of your aorta.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: As a result, a flap called a septum forms between the true aortic channel and the false channel.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: From here, the blood may break through the outer layer of the aortic wall, causing an immediate life-threatening condition, or it may re-enter the aorta through another tear in the intima.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: Over time, a blood clot may form in the false channel.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: An aortic dissection may be classified, according to its location and duration of symptoms.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: In the Stanford system, if you have an aortic dissection in your ascending aorta, it is called a type A dissection.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: All other dissections are called type B.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: The most common symptom of the aortic dissection is sharp or stabbing chest pain, usually sudden and severe.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: If you have a type A dissection, you're most likely to feel pain in your chest.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: If you have a type B dissection, you're more likely to feel pain in your back than your chest.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
text_en: If you have these symptoms, you should seek immediate medical attention, as this condition can be life-threatening.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: Health Care Associated Infections or HAIs are infections you may get in a health care

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: facility while you are receiving treatment for a different condition.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: Bacteria such as methamillicillin-resistant staphlococcus aureus, also known as merse,

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: interococcus species, and claustridium difficile or C-diff are frequently the cause of health

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: care associated infections.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: These infections are a major cause of death around the world.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: One of the most common ways these bacteria get passed from person to person is through

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: lack of proper hand hygiene.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: When people don't clean their hands, they can transfer microbes from one patient to another,

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: or from one part of the body to another on the same patient.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: There are four common types of health care associated infections.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: A surgical wound infection can result when bacteria get into your incision and spread through

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: your tissues, leading to infection.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: A urinary tract infection can occur when you have a catheter in your bladder, which is

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: a tube used to drain your urine.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: Bacteria can enter your urinary system on the catheter, enabling the bacteria to travel

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: into your bladder where they can grow.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: A bloodstream infection can occur when you have a central line in your vein, which is a

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: slender tube used to deliver fluids or medications into a large vein near your heart.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: Bacteria can enter the skin around the insertion site, or directly into your bloodstream,

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: where they can cause infection.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: Numonia can result when you are on a ventilator or breathing machine.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: Bacteria can enter your throat while you are being placed on the ventilator.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: Then you may inhale the bacteria into your lungs, leading to infection that can cause pneumonia.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: Hand hygiene, a simple, low-cost and effective procedure, is the single most important method

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: of preventing health care associated infections.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: All people in health care facilities, including patients, visitors and medical personnel,

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: can help prevent these infections by cleaning their hands frequently.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: Hand hygiene can be performed with soap and running water for 40 to 60 seconds, or by

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: thoroughly rubbing on an alcohol-based hand sanitizer for 20 to 30 seconds.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: If you are receiving health care, you are part of the health care team, and you should

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
text_en: make sure that everyone who comes into contact with you cleans their hands.

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
text_en: A CT scan, also known as a CAT scan or computerized axial tomography, is a painless diagnostic

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: A pressure injury or ulcer is a wound caused by constant pressure on your skin.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: It may also be called a pressure sore, pressure ulcer, bed sore, or de-cubitus ulcer.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: Pressure injuries occur most frequently in areas where your bones are close to the skin,

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: including your heels, ankles, hips, tailbone, or elbows.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: In order to understand how pressure injuries happen, it's important to know about the layers

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: of the skin.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: Your skin is made up of three main layers.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: The top layer called the epidermis is a thin layer that protects your body from the environment.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: The thicker middle layer is called the dermis.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: Its main functions are to supply the epidermis with nutrients, help regulate your body temperature,

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: and provide strength and elasticity to your skin.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: The innermost layer is called the hypodermis, or subcutaneous layer.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: This layer stores fat, which cushions the muscles and bones beneath it.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: In a pressure injury, constant pressure on the skin over a long period of time can compress

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: the blood vessels that supply the layers with the oxygen and nutrients they need to survive.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: If there's not enough blood flow, your skin tissue begins to die and a pressure injury

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: forms.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: If you have a pressure injury, your healthcare provider will classify it based on what can

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: be known about the depth of tissue damage.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: This is called staging.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: In a stage 1 pressure injury, the skin is intact, red, and inflamed.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: The redness is non-blanchable.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: This means it doesn't become pale if you press on it.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: A stage 2 pressure injury appears as a blister or an open sore.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: There is partial thickness loss of the epidermis and dermis.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: The areas surrounding the sore may be red and irritated.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: A stage 3 pressure injury is a crater-like, full thickness loss of skin.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: It extends down to the fat tissue of the hypodermis.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: Stage 4 is the most severe.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: A stage 4 pressure injury is a full thickness loss of tissue.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: It extends through all three layers of skin and into the underlying muscle or bone.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: An unstageable pressure injury is a full thickness loss of tissue.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: It's called unstageable because the injury is covered by dead tissue, making it hard

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: to tell how deep it is.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: And a suspected deep tissue injury appears as a maroon or purple area of the skin.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: It may contain a blister filled with blood.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: It looks like this because of damaged soft tissue underneath your skin.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: To prevent infection in your pressure injury, your doctor will clean your wound using water

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: and a mild soap or saline solution.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: If your wound is severe, your doctor will debris or surgically remove the dead tissue from

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: the pressure injury.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: To protect your wound and keep it from drying out while it heals, your doctor may cover it

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: with a specialized bandage.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: To find out more about pressure injuries, talk to your healthcare provider.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Every time you smoke a cigarette, toxic gases pass into your lungs, then into your blood

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: stream, where they spread to every organ in your body.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: A cigarette is made using the tobacco leaf, which contains nicotine and a variety of other

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: compounds. As the tobacco and compounds burn, they release thousands of dangerous chemicals,

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: including over 40 known to cause cancer.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Cigarette smoke contains the poisonous gases carbon monoxide and nitrogen oxide, as well

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: as trace amounts of cancer-causing radioactive particles.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: All forms of tobacco are dangerous, including cigars, pipes, and smokeless tobacco, such

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: as chewing tobacco and snuff.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Nicotine is an addictive chemical in tobacco. After you inhale tobacco smoke, nicotine flows

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: through the bloodstream to your brain, where it induces a pleasurable feeling.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: When you repeatedly expose your brain to nicotine, it becomes desensitized, making you crave

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: more and more nicotine just to feel normal.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Smoking causes death. People who smoke typically die at an earlier age than non-smokers.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: In fact, one of every five deaths in the United States is linked to cigarette smoking.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: If you smoke your risk of major health problems increases dramatically, including heart disease,

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: heart attack, stroke, lung cancer, and death from chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Smoking causes cardiovascular disease. When nicotine flows through your adrenal glands,

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: it stimulates the release of epinephrine, a hormone that raises your blood pressure.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: In addition, nicotine and carbon monoxide can damage the lining of the inner walls in

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: your arteries. Fatty deposits, called plaque, can build up at these injury sites and become

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: large enough to narrow the arteries and severely reduce blood flow, resulting in a condition

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: called atherosclerosis.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: In coronary artery disease, atherosclerosis narrows the arteries that supply the heart, which

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: reduces the supply of oxygen to your heart muscle, increasing your risk for a heart attack.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Smoking also raises your risk for blood clots, because it causes platelets in your blood

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: to clump together.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Smoking increases your risk for peripheral vascular disease, in which atherosclerotic

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: plaques block the large arteries in your arms and legs.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Smoking can also cause an abdominal aortic aneurysm, which is a swelling or weakening of

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: your aorta where it runs through your abdomen.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Smoking damages two main parts of your lungs.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Your airways also called bronchial tubes, and small air sacs called alveoli.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: With each breath, air travels down your windpipe, called the trachea, and enters your lungs

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: through your bronchial tubes.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Air then moves in two thousands of tiny alveoli, where oxygen from the air moves into your blood

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: stream, and the waste product carbon dioxide moves out of your blood stream.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Tiny hair-like projections, called cilia, line your bronchial tubes and sweep harmful substances

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: out of your lungs.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Cigarette smoke irritates the lining of your bronchial tubes, causing them to swell and make

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: mucus.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Cigarette smoke also slows the movement of your cilia, causing some of the smoke and

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: mucus to stay in your lungs.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: While you're sleeping, some of the cilia recover and start pushing more pollutants and

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: mucus out of your lungs.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: When you wake up, your body attempts to expel this material by coughing repeatedly, a

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: condition known as smokers cough.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Over time, chronic bronchitis develops as your cilia stop working, your airways become

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: clogged with scars and mucus, and breathing becomes difficult.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Your lungs are now more vulnerable to further disease.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Cigarette smoke also damages your LVLI, making it harder for oxygen and carbon dioxide

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: to exchange with your blood.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Over time, so little oxygen can reach your blood that you may develop emphysema, a condition

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: in which you must gasp for every breath and wear an oxygen tube under your nose in order

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: to breathe.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Chronic bronchitis and emphysema are collectively called chronic obstructive pulmonary disease

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: or COPD.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: COPD is a gradual loss of the ability to breathe for which there is no cure.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Cigarette smoke contains at least 40 cancer-causing substances called carcinogens, including cyanide,

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: formaldehyde, benzene, and ammonia.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: In your body, healthy cells grow, make new cells, then die.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Genetic material inside each cell, called DNA, directs this process.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: If you smoke toxic chemicals, can damage the DNA in your healthy cells.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: As a result, your damaged cells create new unhealthy cells, which grow out of control

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: and may spread to other parts of your body.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: The most common cancer in the world is lung cancer, with over a million new cases diagnosed

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: every year.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Harmful chemicals in cigarettes can cause cancer in other parts of your body, such as, in the

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: blood and bone marrow, mouth, larynx, throat, esophagus, stomach, pancreas, kidney, bladder,

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: uterus, and cervix.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Cigarette can cause infertility in both men and women.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: If a woman is pregnant and smokes during pregnancy, she exposes her baby to the cigarette's

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: poisonous chemicals, causing a greater risk of low birth weight, miscarriage, pre-term delivery,

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: still birth, infant death, and sudden infant death syndrome.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Hitting is also dangerous if a mother is breastfeeding.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Nicotine passes to the baby through breast milk, and can cause restlessness, rapid heartbeat,

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: vomiting, interrupted sleep, or diarrhea.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Other health effects of smoking include low bone density and increased risk for hip fracture

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: among women, gum disease often leading to tooth loss and surgery, immune system dysfunction,

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: delayed wound healing, and sexual impotence in men.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients your

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: body needs.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: The pumping sections of the heart are the right atrium, right ventricle, left atrium,

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: and left ventricle.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: In your heart, oxygen poor blood flows from your body through large veins into your

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: right atrium.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: Next, your blood moves into your right ventricle, which contracts, sending blood out of the

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: heart to pick up oxygen from your lungs.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: Oxygen rich blood moves out of your lungs into your left atrium, then moves into your

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: left ventricle.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: Finally, your left ventricle contracts, sending oxygen rich blood out of your heart to your

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: body.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: If you have heart failure, your heart has lost the ability to pump enough blood to meet

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: your body's needs.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: You may have weak or damaged ventricular walls that are not able to push enough blood

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: out of your heart.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: You may also have stiff and thickened ventricular walls that do not allow your heart to fill

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: with enough blood.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: If you have left-sided heart failure, your left ventricle does not deliver enough oxygen-rich

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: blood to your body, making you feel tired and out of breath.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: Your failing left ventricle also increases the blood pressure in the blood vessels between

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: your lungs and left ventricle.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: This increased pressure forces fluid out of your blood and into your lung tissues, which

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: makes it difficult for you to breathe.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: If you have right-sided heart failure, your right ventricle is unable to contract with

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: enough force to push blood to your lungs.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: The result is a build-up of blood in your veins, which causes a build-up of fluid called

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: edema throughout the tissues in your body.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: Over time, heart failure on either side of your heart results in weakened and large

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: ventricles that deliver less blood to your body.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: To make up for the decreased amount of blood, your nervous system releases stress hormones

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: that increase the speed and force of your heart beat.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: Unfortunately, the continued release of these stress hormones makes your heart failure

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: worse because they damage the muscle cells in your ventricles.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: The most common cause of heart failure is coronary artery disease.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: Other causes that damage your heart and lead to heart failure include high blood pressure,

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: diabetes, diseased infected or damaged heart valves, diseased infected or damaged heart

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: muscle, irregular heartbeats called arrhythmias, heart defects, poisons or substance abuse,

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: lung diseases, and breathing problems during sleep called sleep apnea.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: Treatment for heart failure includes medications, lifestyle changes, or in extreme heart failure,

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: heart beat assisting devices or surgery.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: Medications reduce the swelling in your body by increasing the amount of urine produced

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: by your kidneys.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: Ace inhibitors are medications that allow your blood vessels to expand.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: This helps decrease your blood pressure and prevents further damage to your heart by making

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: it easier for your heart to pump blood.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: Beta blockers are medications that block the effects of stress hormones on your heart.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: Although beta blockers slow down your heart beat, they are mainly used to protect your

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: heart muscle from the long-term damage caused by stress hormones.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: You may need to make some lifestyle changes, including exercising on a regular basis,

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: maintaining a healthy weight, quitting smoking, limiting salt and alcohol,

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: and eating a heart-healthy diet.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: Surgery for advanced heart failure include coronary artery bypass surgery to improve blood flow to your heart muscle,

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: heart valve reconstruction surgery, to improve blood flow through your heart,

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: and left ventricle reconstruction surgery to remove damaged heart muscle.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: Surgeries for extreme heart failure include insertion of a device to help your heart pump blood,

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
text_en: and heart transplant.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Your heart is an extraordinary machine.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: During your lifetime, your heart's muscular walls pump blood into blood vessels, branching

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: throughout your body.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Your heart has four chambers.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Two upper chambers, called the left and right atria, and two lower chambers, called

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: the left and right ventricles, contract in a steady rhythm known as your heart beat.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: During a normal heart beat, blood from your tissues and lungs flows into your atria, then

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: into your ventricles.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Inside your heart, called the inter atrial and interventricular septa, help keep the

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: blood on left and right sides from mixing.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Two valves sit like doors between your atria and ventricles to prevent blood from flowing

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: backward into your atria.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: The tricuspid valve opens into your right ventricle, and the mitral valve opens into your

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: left ventricle.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Strong, thin tissues, called corda tendonae, hold your valves in place during the forceful

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: contractions of your ventricles.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Blood leaving the ventricles passes through another set of valves, the pulmonary valve

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: between your right ventricle and pulmonary trunk, and the aortic valve connecting your

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: left ventricle and aorta.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: In order to pump blood more efficiently, your heart muscle, called myocardium, is arranged

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: in a unique pattern.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Three layers of myocardium wrap around the lower part of your heart.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: They twist and tighten in different directions to push blood through your heart.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: When special cells, called pacemaker cells, generate electrical signals inside your heart.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: The heart muscle cells, called myocytes, contract as a group.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Your heart is divided into left and right halves, which work together like a dual pump.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: On the right side of your heart, deoxygenated blood from your body's tissues flows through

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: large veins, called the superior and inferior vena cava, into your right atrium.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Next, the blood moves into your right ventricle, which contracts and sends blood out of your

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: heart to your lungs to gather oxygen and get rid of carbon dioxide.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: On the left side of your heart, oxygen-rich blood from your lungs flows through your

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: pulmonary veins into your left atrium.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: The blood then moves into your left ventricle, which contracts and sends blood out of your

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: heart through the aorta to feed your cells and tissues.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: The first branches off your aorta are the coronary arteries, which supply your heart muscle

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: with oxygen and nutrients.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: At the top of your aorta, arteries branch off to carry blood to your head and arms.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Arteries branching from the middle and lower parts of your aorta, supply blood to the

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: rest of your body.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: Your heart beats an average of 60 to 100 beats per minute.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: In that one minute, your heart pumps about 5 quarts of blood through your arteries,

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
text_en: delivering a steady stream of oxygen and nutrients all over your body.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: In hypoplastic left heart syndrome, the left side of a newborn baby's heart hasn't

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: developed completely.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients that

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: the body needs.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: The pumping sections of the heart are the right atrium, right ventricle, left atrium,

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: and left ventricle.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: In a normal heart, oxygen poor blood flows from the body through large veins, called the

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: superior vena cava and inferior vena cava, into the right atrium.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: Next, blood moves into the right ventricle, which contracts.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: Sending blood to the lungs through the pulmonary artery.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: Blood picks up oxygen in the lungs and returns through the pulmonary veins to the left atrium,

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: then moves into the left ventricle.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: Finally, the left ventricle contracts.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: Sending oxygen-rich blood to the body through the aorta.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: For birth, the heart doesn't send blood to the lungs to get oxygen, since the baby

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: is not breathing yet.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: Instead, all the oxygen the baby needs passes from the mother's blood to the baby through

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: the placenta and a miracle cord.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: In addition, blood from the baby's heart bypasses the lungs in two ways.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: One is through a connection between the pulmonary artery and the aorta, called the ductus

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: arteriosus.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: The other is through an opening in the wall between the two atria, called the foreamen ovale.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: These extra openings allow oxygen-rich blood to bypass the baby's lungs and still circulate

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: through the body.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: Normally, within a day or two after birth, both the ductus arteriosus and the foreamen ovale

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: close.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: Forcing the baby's blood to travel to the lungs to pick up oxygen.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: In hypoplastic left heart syndrome, a newborn baby's heart has severe birth defects that

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: prevent blood from flowing normally to the rest of the body.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: If the body can't get oxygen and nutrients, it can't survive.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: The left ventricle, which is the main pump and the aorta, which is the main pipeline,

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: are smaller than normal, so the baby's body does not receive enough blood.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: If the foreamen ovale and ductus arteriosus are allowed to close as they would normally,

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: blood flow to the body will decrease even more.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: Resulting in a life-threatening situation

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: Symptoms of hypoplastic left heart syndrome include gray or bluish skin color and rapid

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: breathing as the baby tries to get more oxygen to the body.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: Without surgical intervention, this condition is fatal.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: Surgeons will perform three separate operations to treat hypoplastic left heart syndrome.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: The goal of these operations is to improve the flow of oxygen-rich blood to the baby's body.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: The Stage 1 operation, called the Norwood procedure, is performed during the first week

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: after birth.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: During the operation, the surgeon will make three main changes.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: First, the pulmonary artery will be separated from the right and left pulmonary arteries

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: and connected to the aorta.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: A small piece of tubing, called a graft, will be inserted to connect a branch of the aorta

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: and the right pulmonary artery.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: Finally, the surgeon will remove the wall between the right and left atria.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: These changes increase the flow of oxygen-rich blood from the lungs through both atria

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: into the right ventricle, increase the blood flow to the body through the new aorta,

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: and increase the blood flow to the lungs through the graft.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: The Stage 2 operation, called the Glenn procedure, is performed about six months later when

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: the baby is stronger.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: The surgeon will remove the graft connecting the branch of the aorta and the right pulmonary

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: artery.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: To replace the graft's function, the surgeon will disconnect the superior vena-cava from

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: the heart and reconnect it to the right pulmonary artery.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: This connection allows oxygen-poor blood from the head and arms to go directly to the lungs

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: to pick up oxygen.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: The Stage 3 operation, called the Fontan procedure, is performed when the baby is 18 months to

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: three years old.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: The surgeon will disconnect the inferior vena-cava from the heart and reconnect it to the

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: right pulmonary artery with graft tubing.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: This connection allows oxygen-poor blood from the lower body to go directly to the lungs

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: to pick up oxygen.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: The Fontan procedure completes the surgical repair of the heart by achieving these goals.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: The discovery of oxygen-rich blood to the body through the creation of a new and expanded

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: aorta, creation of a new path for oxygen-poor blood to reach the lungs, and prevention of the

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: mixing of oxygen-rich blood and oxygen-poor blood.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: These three operations relieve the life-threatening condition caused by hypoplastic left heart syndrome

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: but do not restore normal circulation through the heart.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: In some cases, a heart transplant may be done if a donor heart is available or the changes

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: from the three surgical procedures aren't working.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: After any of these operations, the child may receive blood thinner medication to prevent

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: the formation of blood clots.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: Those with heart transplants will also need medication to prevent their body from rejecting

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: the new heart.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
text_en: The child may need these medications for the rest of their life.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Your heart pumps blood to your brain through blood vessels in your neck.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: These blood vessels, called arteries, branch into even smaller arteries that bring blood

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: to your brain cells.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Some of these small arteries join each other to form a loop known as the circle of willis.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: The walls of the arteries contain special muscle and elastic layers that help them adapt

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: to the blood pulsing through them.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Sometimes diseases, injuries, or birth defects can cause a weak spot in the wall of the arteries

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: in the circle of willis.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: These weak spots usually form where arteries branch.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Over time, the pressure of blood flowing through the artery stresses the weak spot further.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: The constant blood pressure can cause the weak spot to balloon out and form a bulge called

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: a cerebral aneurysm.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: This cerebral aneurysm is the most common type called a sacular or berry aneurysm.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Over time, the aneurysm may grow and push on nearby brain tissue or nerves.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Blood may also leak from the aneurysm.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: If its wall gets too thin, the aneurysm can burst or rupture and spill high pressure

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: blood into the space around your brain.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: The blood presses on your brain tissue and can cause injury to your brain cells.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Chemicals in the escaped blood can irritate other brain arteries and cause them to narrow.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: As a result, your brain can swell, leading to serious brain injury.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Common symptoms of a ruptured cerebral aneurysm include sudden severe headache, sometimes

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: called a thunder clap headache, problems with your vision such as blurred or double vision,

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: nausea and vomiting, a stiff or painful neck, or confusion.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: An unruptured cerebral aneurysm may not cause symptoms, but large unruptured aneurysms

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: may cause headache and pain near or behind one eye, double or blurred vision, a drooping

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: eyelid and numbness or weakness on one side of your face.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: A ruptured cerebral aneurysm is a medical emergency if you are having symptoms seek immediate

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: medical attention.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: If you have a ruptured cerebral aneurysm, your doctor may recommend surgical clipping

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: or endovascular coiling.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: In surgical clipping, your surgeon will make an opening in your skull, near your aneurysm,

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: and place a small metal clip onto the stem that connects the aneurysm to your artery.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: The clip blocks blood flow into your aneurysm.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: In endovascular coiling, your doctor will put a plastic tube called a catheter into a large

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: artery in your groin.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Using a live X-ray view, your doctor will advance the catheter through your arteries until

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: it reaches the aneurysm.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: Through the catheter, your doctor will insert one or more flexible wires into your aneurysm.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: The wires will coil up and cause a blood clot to form inside your aneurysm, which will

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: block off the aneurysm from the rest of your artery.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: In a similar manner, if you have an unruptured cerebral aneurysm, your doctor may recommend

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: surgical clipping or endovascular coiling.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: In addition, if your aneurysm is small and not causing pain or other symptoms, your doctor

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
text_en: may observe your aneurysm for a period of time before performing either of these procedures.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: High blood pressure or hypertension is a common condition in which the force of blood on

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: the walls of your arteries is often too high.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Arteries are the blood vessels that carry blood away from your heart to supply your tissues

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: with oxygen and nutrients.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: In your heart, two chambers, called ventricles, contract with each heartbeat to push blood

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: to your lungs and through your arteries to your body.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: As blood flows through them, three main factors affect the pressure on your artery walls.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: The first is cardiac output or the amount of blood your ventricles push out of your

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: heart each minute.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Your blood pressure goes up as cardiac output increases.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: The second factor affecting your blood pressure is blood volume or the total amount of blood

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: in your body.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Blood pressure also goes up as blood volume increases.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: The third factor that affects your blood pressure is resistance, which is anything working

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: against the blood flow through your arteries.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Several factors contribute to resistance.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: One resistance factor is the flexibility of your artery wall.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Healthy arteries expand with each heartbeat to help reduce blood pressure on the wall.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Another resistance factor is the diameter of your arteries.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Your body is able to increase the diameter of your arteries to lower your blood pressure

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: or reduce the diameter to raise your blood pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: A third resistance factor is blood viscosity or thickness.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: In your blood, more particles, such as proteins and fat, increase viscosity.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: If your blood is thicker, your blood pressure goes up as your heart works harder to push

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: it through your arteries.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Your blood pressure can be measured with a device called a Sphigmomanometer or blood pressure

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: cuff.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: When your heart beats, the pressure of blood on the walls of your arteries is called systolic

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: When your heart relaxes between beats, pressure on the artery wall is called diastolic pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: While your blood pressure may change throughout the day, it should optimally be less than 120

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
