text_en: millimeters of mercury for systolic pressure and less than 80 millimeters of mercury for

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: diastolic pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: If your systolic pressure frequently stays above 130 or your diastolic pressure frequently

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: stays above 80, you have high blood pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Your time, high blood pressure will damage the walls of your arteries.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Your artery wall may become weak and form an enlargement called an aneurysm or the wall

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: may burst and bleed into the surrounding tissue.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Small tears in your artery wall may attract certain substances in your blood, such as cholesterol,

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: fat and calcium to form a buildup called a plaque.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Blood flow through your artery decreases as the plaque enlarges.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Blood cells can stick to the plaque and form solid clumps called clots, further reducing

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: or completely blocking your blood flow.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Damage to your arteries raises your blood pressure even more by making your heart beat

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: more forcefully.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Artery damage and reduced blood flow lead to conditions such as a stroke, heart attack

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: or kidney disease.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: In most cases, the cause of high blood pressure or hypertension is unknown.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: This type of high blood pressure is called primary or essential hypertension.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Treatment for essential hypertension includes lifestyle changes, such as eating a healthy

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: diet.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: If you are sensitive to the sodium in salt, your doctor may recommend limiting your intake

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: of salt and highly processed foods.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Your doctor may cause your body to retain water, which increases both your blood volume

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: and your blood pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Other lifestyle changes that can reduce blood pressure include avoiding excessive alcohol

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: intake, getting regular exercise, losing weight if you are overweight, and quitting smoking,

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: and your doctor may also recommend medications that act on your kidneys, blood vessels,

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: or heart to help reduce your blood pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Diuretics commonly called water pills cause your kidneys to move more salt and water

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: from your blood into your urine, which reduces your blood volume and pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Beta blockers reduce the workload on your heart by decreasing both the rate of your heart

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: beat and the strength of your heart's contractions.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Several types of drugs act directly or indirectly to reduce your blood pressure by relaxing your

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: blood vessels, which increases their diameter.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: These drugs include ACE inhibitors, angiotensin-2 receptor blockers, calcium channel blockers,

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: and direct acting vasodilators.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
text_en: If you have hyperlipedemia, commonly known as high cholesterol, you have high levels of

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Your doctor may recommend a craniotomy or craniectomy procedure to treat a number of different

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: brain diseases, injuries or conditions.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Your skull is made of bone and serves as a hard protective covering for your brain.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Just inside your skull, three layers of tissue called meninges surround your brain.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: The thick, outermost layer is the duromotor.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: The middle tissue layer is the arachnoid motor.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: And the innermost layer is the pia-motor.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Between the arachnoid motor and the pia-motor is the subarachnoid space, which contains

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: blood vessels and a clear fluid called cerebrospinal fluid.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Blood vessels, called bridging veins, connect the surface of your brain with the duromotor.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Other blood vessels, called cerebral arteries, bring blood to your brain.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Inside your skull, normal brain function requires a delicate balance of pressure between the

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: blood in your blood vessels, the cerebrospinal fluid that surrounds your brain, and your brain

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: tissue.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: This is called normal intracranial pressure.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Increased intracranial pressure may result from brain tumors, head injuries, problems with

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: your blood vessels, or infections in your brain or spinal cord.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: These conditions put pressure on your brain and may cause it to swell or change shape

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: inside your skull, which can lead to serious brain injury.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Your doctor may recommend a craniotomy to remove abnormal brain tissue such as a brain

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: tumor, a sample of tissue by biopsy, a blood clot called a hematoma, excess cerebrospinal

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: fluid, or pus from an infection called an abscess.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: A craniotomy may also be done to relieve brain swelling, stop bleeding called a hemorrhage,

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: repair abnormal blood vessels, repair skull fractures, or repair damaged meninges.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Finally, a craniotomy may also be done to treat brain conditions such as epilepsy,

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: deliver medication to your brain, or implant a medical device such as a deep brain stimulator.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: The most common reason for a craniotomy is to remove a brain tumor.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: For your procedure, you will be given general anesthesia to make you unconscious and pain-free.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: The skin on your scalp will be shaved over the location of the tumor.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: To begin, your surgeon will make an incision in your scalp.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: One or more small holes will be made in your skull.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Then your surgeon will connect the holes to create a circular piece of bone called a bone

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: flap and remove it.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Your surgeon will make an incision in the duromotor to expose your brain.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Then your tumor will be removed.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: The incision in the duromotor will be closed with sutures.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Your surgeon will reattach the bone flap with metal plates and sprues that will remain in your skull for life.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: You may have a variation on a craniotomy called a craniectomy if your brain is very swollen or your skull is infected.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Your surgeon may delay reattaching the bone flap until a later procedure to allow time for the swelling to go down.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: At the end of either a craniotomy or a craniectomy, your scalp incision will be closed with staples.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: And your head will be wrapped in bandages.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: If you had a craniectomy, you will have another procedure called a cranioplasty at a later date after the swelling goes down.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: At that time, your surgeon will reattach your bone flap with metal plates and sprues.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Then close the skin incision with staples.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: After either procedure, you will stay in the hospital for several days.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: Your caregiver will check your brain function by asking you questions and shining a light in your eyes.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
text_en: You may be given medication to prevent complications.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: If you have acute coronary syndrome, you have one or more conditions caused by a blockage

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: of blood flow to your heart muscle.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: This is a medical emergency because you may be having a heart attack, a condition in which

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: your heart muscle begins to die.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients your

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: body needs.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: The main pumping chamber of your heart is the left ventricle.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: When your left ventricle contracts, it sends oxygen-rich blood to your body through

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: a large artery called the aorta.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Connected to your aorta are small arteries called coronary arteries.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Blood flows from your aorta through the coronary arteries to supply your heart muscle with

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: oxygen and nutrients.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: If you have acute coronary syndrome, blood flow through your coronary arteries is severely

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: reduced or completely blocked.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: One possible cause of reduced blood flow is atherosclerosis.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: In this condition, a build-up of a fatty substance called plaque can narrow your coronary

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: arteries.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: If this plaque ruptures, a blood clot can form and block the artery.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: A blood clot is the most common cause of coronary artery blockage.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Other less common causes of reduced blood flow include coronary artery spasm or dissection.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: In a coronary artery spasm, triggers such as drugs, smoking, cold weather, and extreme

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: stress or emotions can cause a temporary and sudden tightening of a coronary artery.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: During a coronary artery dissection, the inside wall of one of your coronary arteries separates

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: which can block blood flow.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Regardless of the cause, a blockage in either coronary artery prevents the oxygen and nutrients

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: in your blood from reaching the part of your heart supplied by the artery.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: As a result, heart muscle in that area starts to die.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Death of part of your heart muscle is called a heart attack.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: It's also known as a myocardial infarction or MI.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: A blocked coronary artery may also cause you to feel sudden pain, discomfort, tightening,

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: or a burning sensation in your chest called angina.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: This pain may extend to your upper abdomen, shoulders, arms, neck, and lower jaw.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: If you have angina when you're at rest or frequent angina that prevents even moderate

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: physical activity, you have unstable angina which is the main symptom of acute coronary

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: syndrome.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Other symptoms of acute coronary syndrome include shortness of breath, dizziness, nausea,

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: and sweating.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: If you've had a heart attack or have other types of acute coronary syndrome, your doctor

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: may prescribe oxygen therapy to get more oxygen into your blood.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: You may take aspirin or other prescription blood thinner drugs to prevent blood clots.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Thrombolytics, also known as clot buster drugs, may be used to break up any existing blood

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: clots.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Cards such as nitroglycerin and morphine will relax your coronary arteries and relieve

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: the pain of angina.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: You may also receive drugs called beta blockers that slow down your heart and reduce its need

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: for oxygen.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Your doctor may also recommend immediate surgical procedures such as coronary angioplasty, in

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: which a balloon-tipped catheter inflates inside your blocked coronary artery to open it.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: After inflating, the balloon catheter may leave behind a mesh-like device called a stent

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: to hold your artery open.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Or you may have a coronary artery bypass graft or cabbage.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Cigilage is a surgical procedure in which the blocked areas of the coronary arteries are

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: bypassed with veins or artificial graft material.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
text_en: Seek treatment immediately if you have the symptoms of acute coronary syndrome.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: A varicoseal is a group of swollen blood vessels called veins in a man's scrotum.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: The male reproductive system includes the testicles, also known as testes, which are two glands

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: inside a pouch of skin called the scrotum.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: The testicles normally produce and store reproductive cells called sperm.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: They also produce a hormone called testosterone.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: Testosterone is important for sperm production, muscle strength, and sex drive.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: The scrotum is outside the body, which keeps the testicles cool enough to make sperm.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: Each testicle is suspended inside the scrotum by a structure called the spermatic cord.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: Inside each cord are blood vessels, and a tube that sperm travel through called the vas deferens.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: The testicular artery is the blood vessel that brings most of the blood to the testicle.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: Most blood leaves the testicle through a group of veins called the Pampiniform Plexus.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: The blood also leaves the testicle through smaller veins next to the vas deferens that bypass the Pampiniform Plexus.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: From the Pampiniform Plexus, blood flows into the testicular veins.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: A series of one-way valves inside the testicular veins prevent blood from flowing backward into the testicles.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: A varicoseal may begin to form when the valves inside the testicular vein are damaged or don't work properly.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: The valves may not close completely, allowing blood to flow backward inside the vein to the testicle.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: In addition, since the left testicular vein is straighter and longer than the right testicular vein,

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: there is a greater downward pressure of the blood inside the left vein.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: Another problem may occur where your left testicular vein connects to a larger vein coming from your kidney,

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: called the left renal vein.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: An artery to the intestines may squeeze the left renal vein, causing blood to back up in the left testicular vein.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: This is called nutcracker syndrome.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: As a result of any of these problems, a varicoseal can form in the left Pampiniform Plexus when blood begins to back up in it.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: The blood pooling in these veins causes them to become swollen.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: Depending on the severity of the varicoseal, the pooled blood may raise the temperature around the testicle.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: This higher temperature can make it harder for the testicle to produce sperm.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: This condition may decrease male fertility, which is the ability to get a woman pregnant.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: Over time, a varicoseal may also decrease the level of testosterone made in the testicles.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: Low testosterone can lead to decreased energy and muscle strength, as well as sexual problems.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: A varicoseal doesn't usually cause symptoms or require treatment.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: However, if a varicoseal causes pain, discomfort, or problems with fertility,

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: the most common treatment is either an in-winnel or sub-in-winnel microsurgical varicoselectomy.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: To begin, the surgeon will make a small incision low on the groin for a sub-in-winnel varicoselectomy,

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: or slightly higher for an in-winnel varicoselectomy.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: Then, the spermatic cord will be pulled out through the incision.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: The surgeon will open the outer tissue of the spermatic cord and separate the varicoseal from

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: the rest of the tissue. Small metal clips will be placed on these veins.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: Then, the veins will be cut.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: After these veins are cut, blood will still be able to leave the testicle through the

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: smaller veins on the vas deferens. These veins are less likely to become swollen.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: The surgeon will finish the procedure by placing the spermatic cord back through the skin

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: incision and closing the incision with sutures and skin closure tape.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
text_en: Other procedures that may be done to repair a varicoseal include a laparoscopic varicoseal repair

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: The human body contains organs such as bones, the brain, heart, and reproductive organs.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: The basic cells that give rise to all of the different cells in these organs are called

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: stem cells.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: One type of stem cell is an egg cell from a woman that has been fertilized by a sperm

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: from a man.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: This single cell, called a zygote, is the first cell in a developing human being.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: It's also called a totipotent stem cell because it can form any type of cell in the body,

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: as well as the umbilical cord and placenta.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: After a zygote has divided a few times, it becomes an early stage embryo called a blastocyst.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: Embryonic stem cells come from the cells inside the blastocyst.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: Embryonic stem cells are pleuripotent.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: This means they can form any cell in the body, but not the cells in the umbilical cord or placenta.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: In the lab, embryonic stem cells are donated from leftover embryos created during

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: in vitro fertilization.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: In vitro fertilization is a procedure to help a woman become pregnant.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: Another type of stem cell is an adult stem cell.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: Small groups of these cells are found in some organs such as the skin, after birth,

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: and into adulthood.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: Adult stem cells are multipotent.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: This means they can only become a few different cell types related to the organ where they're

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: found.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: For example, the skin contains a small number of adult stem cells that can divide to create

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: new skin adult stem cells.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: Or they can become more specialized skin cells to replace those that are lost due to cell

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: aging or damage.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: In the lab, scientists can now induce or cause a regular body cell such as a skin cell

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: to change into a pleuripotent stem cell.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: Like embryonic stem cells, these induced pleuripotent stem cells can become any type of cell

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: in the body.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: Most study stem cells to learn how and why they become many different types of cells.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: In the future, these cells may be used to regrow tissues and organs that have been damaged

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: by injury or disease.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: Stem cell therapy is a procedure that uses stem cells to treat a disease or condition.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: Currently, stem cell therapy only treats diseases and cancers of the blood.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: In leukemia, for example, the patient's bone marrow makes many abnormal white blood cells

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: that can't do their job to fight infection.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: Over time, these abnormal cells crowd out the production of healthy white blood cells.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: In stem cell therapy for leukemia called hematopoetic stem cell transplantation, a doctor

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: will take a sample of blood or bone marrow from the patient or a donor.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: In some cases, donor blood may come from a baby's umbilical cord after it's born.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: These tissue samples contain healthy hematopoetic or blood-forming stem cells.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: In addition, doctors will give the patient chemotherapy drugs or use radiation to kill

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: the abnormal white blood cells and their stem cells.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: Once the abnormal cells are gone, the doctor will transplant the healthy hematopoetic stem

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: cells from the tissue samples into the patient.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: These healthy stem cells will make new blood cells, including normal white blood cells,

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
text_en: which will allow the body to fight off infections.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: Depending on activity level, the heart beats about 60 to 100 times per minute.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: It may be higher during exercise or lower at rest.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: A normal heart rate and rhythm ensures the delivery of oxygen-rich blood to all of the

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: body's organs, such as the brain and lungs.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: A group of cells in the heart, called the cardiac conduction system, uses electrical

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: impulses to control the speed and rhythm of each heartbeat.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: Each heartbeat starts in the right atrium in the sinusoidal atrial or SA node, then spreads

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: through the walls of the heart chambers, called the atria, and ventricles, causing them

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: to contract.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: This process repeats with each heartbeat.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: Problems with the cardiac conduction system cause the heart to have an abnormal rhythm

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: called aniridhmia.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: This may cause an irregular pulse.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: Aridhmia may happen in the atria or ventricles.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: Ritms of aridmias include fibrillation, which is an irregular heartbeat rhythm, tachycardia,

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: which is a fast heartbeat of more than 100 beats per minute, and bradycardia, which is a

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: slow heartbeat of less than 60 beats per minute.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: Fibrillation is the most common type of aridhmia.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: Random impulses cause the atria to fibrillate or twitch rapidly and randomly.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: Tachycardia in the atria is called supraventricular tachycardia.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: In focal atrial tachycardia, small areas within the atria wall start or pass along impulses

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: that cause the atria to contract rapidly, but with irregular rhythm.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: In atrial flutter, larger areas within the atrial wall start or pass along impulses

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: that cause the atria to contract rapidly, but with irregular rhythm.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: Tachycardia may also happen in the ventricles with rapid and regular contractions.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: The body may not receive enough blood because the ventricles contract before completely

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: filling with blood.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: The most serious aridhmia is ventricular fibrillation, where many random impulses fire rapidly within

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: the ventricular walls.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: In ventricular fibrillation, the ventricles are quivering instead of beating.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: This is a medical emergency because the heart cannot effectively pump blood to the body

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: or itself.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: Sometimes problems with the SA node or problems with the pathway of the electrical impulses

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: to the ventricles can cause the slow heartbeat in bradycardia.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: If the heartbeat is too slowly, the body may not receive enough oxygen-rich blood.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: Depending on the type of aridhmia, a doctor may recommend one or more of the following

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: treatments.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: Life style changes, such as eating a heart-healthy diet, exercising, and quitting smoking.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: Medication, such as anti-aridmic drugs and beta blockers, catheter ablation, where thin

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: wires inserted into the heart, destroy the tissue causing the aridhmia with hot or cold

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: energy, and implantable devices such as a pacemaker or defibrillator to correct the pace

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
text_en: or rhythm of the heart.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: Depending on activity level, the heart beats about 60 to 100 times per minute.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: It may be higher during exercise or lower at rest.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: A normal heart rate and rhythm ensures the delivery of oxygen-rich blood to all of the

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: body's organs, such as the brain and lungs.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: A group of cells in the heart, called the cardiac conduction system, uses electrical

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: impulses to control the speed and rhythm of each heartbeat.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: An abnormal heart rate or rhythm, called an arrhythmia, occurs when there's a problem

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: with the heart's conduction system.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: Taca-cardia is a type of arrhythmia where the heart beats too fast.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: Fibrillation is a type of arrhythmia where the heart beats irregularly and may be too

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: fast.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: For certain types of arrhythmias, a catheter ablation procedure may be necessary to stop the

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: heart tissue from causing the arrhythmia.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: After numbing a small area in the groin with a needle, the doctor will insert a short

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: hollow tube called a catheter sheath into the femoral vein.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: Next, a long flexible tube called a catheter will be inserted through the sheath.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: The doctor will guide the catheter to the heart through a blood vessel that goes to the

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: heart, called the inferior vena-cava.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: The location and progress of the catheter will be monitored.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: When the catheter reaches the heart, the doctor will guide it to the area that is causing

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: the arrhythmia.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: The doctor will find the problem area is using a 3D map of the electrical activity of

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: the patient's heart.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: The tip of the catheter will emit either hot energy or cold energy to ablate the tissue

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: in this area.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: The patient makes the treated area stop working.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: For an atrial arrhythmia, a doctor will ablate the atrial tissue causing it.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: If the affected tissues are small, well-defined areas, the procedure is called focal ablation.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: Or if the affected tissues are larger areas with more complex rhythm disturbances, the doctor

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: may perform a procedure called ablation remodeling.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: Both types of ablation restore normal electrical impulses and prevent an arrhythmia from happening.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: If the cause of the arrhythmia is in the ventricle, the doctor can do either focal ablation

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
text_en: or ablation remodeling to treat more complex arrhythmias of the ventricle.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: The heart has four muscular pumping chambers, the right atrium, the left atrium, the right

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: ventricle, and the left ventricle.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: The left ventricle pumps blood out through a large artery called the aorta to supply

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: the body with the oxygen and nutrients it needs.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: When left-sided heart failure, the left ventricle is not able to pump enough blood to the body.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: The two types of left-handed heart failure are diastolic, where the left ventricle can't

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: relax enough to fill with blood, and more commonly systolic, where the left ventricle

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: can't contract strongly enough to push blood to the body.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: In severe or end-stage heart failure, the heart is getting weaker and doesn't respond

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: to medication.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: For left-sided end-stage systolic heart failure, a doctor may recommend an implantable left

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: ventricular assist device, or LVAD, to help the heart pump enough blood to the body.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: An LVAD is a small mechanical pump implanted inside the chest or abdomen that takes over

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: the job of the weak left ventricle.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: Blood flows from the left ventricle through an inflow tube to the pump.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: The pump pushes the blood through an outflow tube into the aorta.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: From here, the blood travels normally from the aorta to the rest of the body.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: A cable, called a drive line, connects the LVAD pump inside the chest to a control unit

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: located on the outside of the body.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: The control unit is a small computer that controls the functions of the pump and alerts

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: the patient if there are any problems.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: The pump and control unit receive power through a cord connected to a battery pack.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: Finally, the control unit and battery pack are held in a lightweight patient pack.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: An LVAD may be a temporary treatment until a heart transplant can be performed, or if

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: the patient isn't able to have a heart transplant, an LVAD may be used for destination therapy,

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: which is long-term treatment.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: You or someone you know may have been diagnosed with high triglycerides.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: This video will show you what they are and how their levels are measured.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: High triglycerides can have harmful effects on your body.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: Triglycerides are a type of fat or lipid found in your blood.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: Your body uses triglycerides for energy.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: One place triglycerides come from is your liver.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: Another is from the food you eat.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: Foods that may raise your triglycerides include fatty foods such as hot dogs and tropical oils,

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: refined carbohydrates such as french fries, foods high in simple sugars such as donuts,

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: and alcohol.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: So how do triglycerides get to the cells that need them?

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: Glycerides in food are digested and eventually reach your liver.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: In the liver, both the triglycerides from your food and those made in your liver are

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: packaged together with proteins.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: Each resulting molecule is called a lipoprotein.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: The lipoproteins travel throughout the body in your bloodstream.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: They deliver triglycerides to your cells which use them for energy.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: Or if you have more than you need, fat cells store the triglycerides as body fat.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: If you are 20 years of age or older, the National Institutes of Health recommends you have

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: a blood test every 5 years.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: The test is called a fasting lipoprotein profile.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: During this test, a blood sample will be taken from your arm after you have not eaten

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: for 9-12 hours.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: This blood test can reveal whether your triglycerides fall into a healthy range.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: For most people, fasting triglycerides should be less than 150 mg per desoliter.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: 500 or higher puts you into the very high range.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: This means there is too much fat in your blood.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: Very high triglycerides can increase your risk of pancreatitis and other cardiovascular

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: events like heart disease.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: Ask your doctor what your triglyceride level should be.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: In many cases, lifestyle changes can help maintain healthy triglyceride levels.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: These changes include eating a diet low in sweets, refined carbohydrates and alcohol,

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: eating regular exercise, managing your weight and not smoking.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: Medications can also help treat impaired triglyceride levels.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: If your doctor prescribed medication to treat high triglycerides, it is important to take

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
text_en: it as directed and report any side effects you may have.

title: ANH15145 LDL and HDL Cholesterol
text_en: If you or someone you know has been diagnosed with high cholesterol, it's important to understand

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
text_en: If you or someone you know has been diagnosed with high cholesterol, it's important to keep

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: If you are watching this video, your healthcare provider has asked you or someone you know

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: to get a blood test to measure your cholesterol levels.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: The National Institutes of Health cholesterol treatment guidelines recommend you have a blood

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: test every five years if you are twenty years or older.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: The test, called a fasting lipoprotein profile, measures your levels of total cholesterol

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: LDL, also known as bad cholesterol HDL, also known as good cholesterol

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: and a type of fat in your blood called triglycerides.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: During this test, a blood sample will be taken after you have not eaten for nine to twelve

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: hours.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: Helping is important to ensure accurate results for this test.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: The total cholesterol goal should be less than 200mg per deciliter.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: For most people, an ideal HDL or good cholesterol should be 60 or higher.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: An ideal LDL or bad cholesterol should be less than 100.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: And fasting triglycerides should be less than 150.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: If you have high blood cholesterol levels, you have a greater risk of heart disease.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: In heart disease, blood vessels called coronary arteries become narrowed or blocked by a

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: waxy substance containing cholesterol, called plaque.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: For time, plaque may reduce or block the flow of oxygen-rich blood to your heart muscle

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: and cause a heart attack.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: There are many known risk factors for heart disease.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: Risk factors you can control include high blood cholesterol and triglycerides, high blood

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: pressure, diabetes, pre-diabetes, being overweight or obese.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: Smoking, lack of physical activity, an unhealthy diet, and stress.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: Risk factors you can't control include age, gender, and having a family history of heart

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: disease.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: If you already have heart disease and multiple or severe risk factors, your doctor may lower

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: your LDL goal to less than 70.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: Your specific goals may vary depending on your health situation.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
text_en: Risk your doctor what your lipid goals should be.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: A healthcare professional can measure your blood pressure to find out if you have high blood pressure.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: This video will help you understand what the numbers in your blood pressure mean.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: Your blood pressure can be measured with a blood pressure cuff called a sphigmomanometer.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: When your heart beats, the pressure of blood on the walls of your arteries is called systolic pressure.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: When your heart relaxes between beats, pressure on the artery wall is called diastolic pressure.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: Your blood pressure may change throughout the day.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: However, optimal systolic pressure should be less than 120 and optimal diastolic pressure should be less than 80.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: If your systolic pressure frequently stays above 130 or your diastolic pressure frequently stays above 80, you have high blood pressure.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: Your specific goals may vary depending on your health situation.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
text_en: Ask your doctor what your blood pressure goals should be.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: This video will help you understand how blood pressure is controlled in your body.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: Blood pressure is the amount of force caused by blood pressing against the walls of your arteries.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: It is tightly controlled to ensure sufficient blood reaches all parts of your body.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: Arteries are blood vessels that carry blood away from your heart to your tissues.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: This blood supplies your tissues with the oxygen and nutrients they need to function.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: In your heart there are two lower chambers called ventricles which contract with each heart beat.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: The right ventricle pushes oxygen poor blood to your lungs to pick up oxygen

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: and the left ventricle pushes oxygen rich blood through your arteries to your body.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: As blood flows through your arteries four main factors affect the pressure on the artery walls.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: The first is the amount of blood your ventricles push out of your heart each minute, also known as cardiac output.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: Your blood pressure goes up as cardiac output increases.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: The second factor affecting your blood pressure is the total amount of blood in your body or blood volume.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: Blood pressure also goes up as blood volume increases.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: The third factor that affects your blood pressure is resistance.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: Resistance is anything working against the blood flow through your arteries.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: Resistance is mainly affected by the width of your arteries.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: Your body is able to increase the width of your arteries to lower your blood pressure.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: Or it can reduce the width to raise your blood pressure.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: The fourth factor that affects your blood pressure is viscosity.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: Viscosity is how thick your blood is.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: In your blood more particles such as proteins and fat increase viscosity.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: If your blood is thicker your blood pressure goes up as your heart works harder to push it through your arteries.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: If you have questions about high blood pressure or any medications you have been prescribed speak with your doctor.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
text_en: It is important to take your medications as directed and report any side effects you have.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: If you or someone you know has high blood pressure, this video will help you understand what it is and why it's important to keep it under control.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Over time, high blood pressure will damage the walls of your arteries. This damage can lead to life-threatening conditions.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: For example, an artery wall may become weak and form an enlarged area called an aneurysm. The wall may burst and bleed into the tissue around it.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: In another example, damage to an artery wall may attract certain substances in your blood, such as cholesterol, fat and calcium. They may form a buildup called a plaque.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: As the plaque gets bigger, blood flow in the artery is reduced.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Blood cells can stick to the plaque and form solid clumps called clots.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Blood clots further reduce or completely block your blood flow.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: If this happens in your brain, it can lead to a stroke.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: If it happens in your heart, it can lead to a heart attack.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: In another example, damage to the arteries in your kidneys may reduce their ability to work properly. This can lead to kidney disease.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: In a final example, damage to your arteries makes your heart beat even harder, which raises your blood pressure even more and can lead to heart failure.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: If you have questions about high blood pressure or any medications you have been prescribed for it, speak with your doctor.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: It is important to take your medications as directed and report any side effects you have.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: If you or someone you know has high blood pressure, this video will show you some ways to manage it.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: In most cases, the cause of high blood pressure or hypertension is unknown.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: This type of high blood pressure is called primary or essential hypertension.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: Treatment for it includes lifestyle changes such as eating a healthy diet.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: If you are sensitive to the sodium insult, your doctor may tell you to eat less salt and processed foods.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: Sodium may cause your body to retain water.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: This increases the amount of your blood and makes your blood pressure go up.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: Other lifestyle changes can lower your blood pressure. They include not drinking too much alcohol.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: Getting regular exercise.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: Losing weight if you need to.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: And quitting smoking.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: Your doctor may also recommend medications that act on your kidneys,

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: blood vessels, or heart to help lower your blood pressure.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: Diuretics commonly called water pills cause your kidneys to move more salt and water from your blood into your urine.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: This reduces both the amount of your blood and your blood pressure.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: Beta blockers reduce the workload on your heart in two ways.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: They slow your heart beat and lower the force of each beat.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: Several types of drugs lower your blood pressure by relaxing your blood vessels.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: This makes them open up wider.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: These drugs include ACE inhibitors, angiotensin-2 receptor blockers, calcium channel blockers,

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: and direct acting vasodilators.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: It is important to stay on your medications as directed even if you are feeling better.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: Do not go off your medication unless your doctor tells you to.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
text_en: If you have any questions about any medications you have been prescribed, speak with your doctor.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: If you have recently been treated for a heart attack, a condition caused by a blockage

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: of blood flow to your heart muscle, this video will help you understand the condition and

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: its treatment.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: your body needs.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: The main pumping chamber of your heart is the left ventricle.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: In your left ventricle contract, it sends oxygen-rich blood to your body through a large

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: artery called the aorta.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: Connected to your aorta are small arteries, called coronary arteries.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: Blood flows from your aorta through the coronary arteries to supply your heart muscle with

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: oxygen and nutrients.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: Using your heart attack, blood flow through one of your coronary arteries may have been

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: severely reduced or completely blocked.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: Your reduced blood flow may have been caused by a buildup of a fatty substance called plaque

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: in your coronary arteries.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: If this plaque became disrupted, a blood clot might form and severely worsen the narrowing

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: or lead to a sudden complete blockage stopping blood flow down the artery.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: A blockage in your coronary arteries prevented the oxygen and nutrients in your blood from

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: reaching the part of your heart supplied by the artery.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: As a result, heart muscle in that area started to die.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: Damage to part of your heart muscle is called a heart attack.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: It's also known as a myocardial infarction, or MI.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: Your heart doctor may have recommended a procedure to help open the blockage and improve

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: blood flow to the damaged area.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: The procedure you had may have been a coronary angioplasty.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: During a coronary angioplasty, a balloon-tipped catheter inflates inside your blocked coronary

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: artery to open it.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: The procedure may have involved placing a stent to help prop the artery open.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: This is usually a thin metal mesh that acts as a scaffold.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: It's important to know that while the stent opens up an artery in your heart, it will

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: not help with plaque buildup that may be in other arteries in your body.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: Or you may have had a coronary artery bypass graft or cabbage.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: Cabbage is a surgical procedure in which the blocked areas of the coronary arteries are

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: bypassed with veins or other arteries from the body.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: Before you left the hospital, your healthcare provider most likely prescribed several medications.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: Your medication may include the following.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: Oral antiplatelet therapy helps prevent platelets from sticking together and forming new blood

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: clots.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: If you have a stent, this medication helps prevent clots from forming inside it.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: You may have also received drugs called beta blockers that help lower your heart rate

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: and blood pressure.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: Drugs such as angiotensin converting enzyme or ACE inhibitors, angiotensin receptor blockers

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: or ARBs, and calcium channel blockers also work to lower your blood pressure if needed.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: And you may have been prescribed statins along with a low fat diet to lower your cholesterol.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: These drugs work by reducing the amount of cholesterol made in your liver.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: They also help your liver pull cholesterol out of your blood.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: If statins don't work well or have serious side effects, your healthcare provider may

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: prescribe non-statin medications.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: They work in different ways to lower your cholesterol.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: It is important to stay on your medications as your physician prescribed, even if you

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: are feeling better.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
text_en: Do not go off your medication unless the healthcare professional that prescribed them tells you to.

title: ANH16181 Heart Failure Overview - Basic
text_en: This video will help you understand what heart failure is.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: Magnetic resonance imaging, commonly known as MRI, helps doctors find, watch, and treat medical problems.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: MRI uses very strong magnets, radio waves, and a computer to make pictures of the inside of the body.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: MRI pictures are more detailed than those made with other methods.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: There are many reasons to have MRI. MRI may find bleeding, such as bleeding in the brain.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: Brain changes as in stroke.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: Problems with your back, such as a herniated disc.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: And an injury inside the body, such as a torn anterior cruciate ligament.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: Before MRI, you will remove all metal objects.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: And it's not safe to have MRI with some medical implants.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: You should tell the MRI technologist about any metal, device, or implant you may have in your body, such as a pacemaker.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: To prepare for MRI, you will lie on a table just outside the scanner.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: MRI scanners are very noisy, so you will probably wear earplugs or headphones.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: Different equipment, such as pillows and straps, will help you stay in the correct position.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: Once you are ready, the table will slide into the scanner.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: Most often, closed scanners, such as this one, are used.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: The area inside is very narrow.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: In some areas, open scanners are available.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: This scanner is open on the sides, so you won't be completely surrounded by the machine.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: However, the pictures may not be as good as with closed scanners.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: For either type of scanner, a technologist will operate it from the next room.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: You will be able to talk to each other through a two-way speaker.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: During the scan, magnets in the machine will create a strong magnetic field. This will not harm you.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: Then, the scanner will send radio waves through your body. This will not harm you.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: When the radio waves are turned off, the scanner will pick up energy signals from your body.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: These signals are used to make the pictures.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: During the scan, you will have to stay very still, so the images will be clear.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: You may be asked to hold your breath for a short time.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: MRIs may take 30 to 45 minutes or longer to complete.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: In some cases, you may get contrast dye with your MRI.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: The dye helps make clearer pictures.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: After the MRI, you can resume your normal activities.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
text_en: If you received contrast dye, the technologist may ask you to drink plenty of water or other healthy drinks to help your kidneys remove the dye from your body.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: Varicose veins are enlarged, twisted veins.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: They usually occur in your legs.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: Varicose veins don't usually cause problems, but if they're painful or you don't like the

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: way they look, you may want to seek treatment.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: Treatment may also help prevent blood clots from forming in the event you have a future

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: surgical procedure, such as a total hip replacement.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: Some patients may have discolored skin, throbbing, aching or cramping, itching, recurrent skin

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: infections, bleeding, or ulcers.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: Most cases of varicose veins can be treated with the following procedures.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: Flare therapy, surface treatments using laser or microelectric devices, phlebectomy, and

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: endovineous ablation.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: These procedures can be done in your doctor's office, require no large cuts, and have a

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: short recovery period.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: You may need more than one treatment over several weeks to see results.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: Most larytherapy works best on smaller veins and spider veins.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: In this procedure, a chemical is injected into the vein.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: The chemical irritates and scars the vein.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: This causes the vein to close off and fade away.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: Surface laser and microelectric treatments are used to treat smaller veins near the skin

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: surface.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: In these procedures, the heat from small bursts of laser light or electric current make

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: the vein fade away.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: A phlebectomy is also used to treat smaller veins near the skin surface.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: During a phlebectomy, a tiny cut is made in the skin over the vein.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: Then the vein is pulled out and removed.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: Endovineous ablation is a procedure to treat deeper varicose veins.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: In this procedure, a flexible tube called a catheter is inserted through a small cut into

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: the vein.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: The catheter is used to send heat from either laser light or radio waves into the vein.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: The heat closes off the vein and causes it to fade away.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: On rare occasions, your healthcare provider may advise a more invasive procedure, such

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: as vein stripping.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: It's usually performed in a hospital and may require more recovery time.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: Only two or three cuts are made in the skin over the damaged vein.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: Then the vein is tied off and pulled out through the lower cut.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: As with any procedure, there are risks as well as benefits.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: Talk to your healthcare provider to see what type of varicose vein treatment may work

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
text_en: best for you.

title: ANH18207 Cardiovascular Conditions and Type 2 Diabetes
text_en: It is common for people with high blood pressure and abnormal lipid levels to also have type

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: People who have diabetes have a higher chance of developing many health problems, including

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: heart disease.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: This video will show you how heart disease can develop, how it affects your health, and

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: what you can do to help reduce your risk of heart disease.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: If you have diabetes, it means your blood glucose or blood sugar is elevated.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: Over time, high blood sugar can damage the blood vessels of your heart and other organs,

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: leading to other health problems.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: This means the longer you have diabetes, the greater your risk for heart disease.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: There are different types of heart disease, including coronary heart disease, cardiomyopathy,

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: arrhythmias, and heart failure.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: Coronary heart disease is a build-up of a cholesterol-filled substance called plaque inside the blood

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: vessels of your heart.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: The build-up of plaque in coronary heart disease can increase your risk of having a heart

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
