title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: attack.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: cardiomyopathy refers to diseases of your heart muscle.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: Cardiomyopathy may lead to abnormal heartbeats called arrhythmias.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: Cardiomyopathy may also lead to heart failure.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: This means your weakened heart can't pump enough blood to meet your body's needs.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: Even both diabetes and heart disease can affect you in many ways.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: You will have a higher risk for other serious health problems such as a heart attack, stroke,

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: and even death.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: It may be harder for you to go about your daily activities.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: You may need help taking care of yourself.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: You may also need to take more medications.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: Living with these conditions may cause depression.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: The good news is that there are things you can do to help lower your risk for heart disease.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: For example, you may need to make changes to your lifestyle.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: If you smoke, get the help you need to quit.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: Both smoking and diabetes can narrow your blood vessels which can increase your chance of

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: developing heart disease or stroke.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: Maintain or get to a healthy weight.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: Follow a healthy eating plan and get regular physical activity.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: Check with your healthcare provider about the right amount of physical activity for you.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: Make sure you work with your healthcare provider to control your blood sugar, cholesterol,

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: and blood pressure.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: This includes taking all medications prescribed by your healthcare provider, including insulin

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
text_en: and pills to help lower blood sugar, blood pressure, and cholesterol.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: This video will help you understand what a stroke is and the risk for having one.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: Normally, blood vessels called arteries carry blood to the brain.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: The brain needs a constant flow of blood to get the oxygen and nutrients it needs to work properly.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: During a stroke, also known as a brain attack, blood flow to part of the brain has stopped.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: The most common type of stroke is called ischemic stroke.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: It can happen in an artery where a fatty substance called plaque has made the artery narrow.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: In one type of ischemic stroke called a thrombotic stroke, the plaque can break apart and form a blood clot

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: that blocks the flow of blood to the brain.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: In another type of ischemic stroke called an embolic stroke, a blood clot forms in another area of the body and travels through the blood stream.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: If it reaches an artery near or in the brain, it can block the flow of blood.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: When an artery to part of the brain is blocked during a stroke, oxygen and nutrients in the blood can't get to those brain cells.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: The cells start to die within minutes.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: A less common type of stroke is called a hemorrhagic stroke.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: It occurs when a weakened blood vessel breaks open and leaks blood into the brain.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: This blocks blood flow to the brain and the leaking blood puts pressure on the brain tissue, which damages it.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: In a transient ischemic attack or TIA, a clot blocks the flow of blood to the brain.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: But it's different from the other types of stroke because the clot breaks up after a short time.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: Transient ischemic attacks usually don't cause any permanent damage.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: High blood pressure is the main risk factor for any type of stroke.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: Other risk factors include diabetes, certain types of heart disease, smoking, high cholesterol,

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: and having previous transient ischemic attacks.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: It's important to know these signs and symptoms of a stroke, a sudden numbness or weakness in your face, arms and legs, especially if it's on one side of your body.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: Sudden confusion and trouble speaking or being unable to understand others speaking.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: Sudden trouble seeing out of one or both eyes.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: Sudden dizziness, loss of balance or being unable to walk.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: And a sudden severe headache.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: Call 911 right away if you think you or someone else may be having a stroke.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: To find out more about your risk for having a stroke, talk to your healthcare provider.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Petrology of fallow is a birth defect that changes normal blood flow through a baby's

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: The treatments for tetralogyofollow are surgical procedures to help fix the following four

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: heart problems.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: A ventricular septal defect, or VSD, is a hole in the wall between the two ventricles.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: An overriding aorta happens when the aorta is located directly over the ventricular

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: septal defect.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: This means the aorta is connected to both the left and right ventricles instead of its

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: normal attachment to only the left ventricle.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: In pulmonary stenosis, the main pulmonary artery is narrowed, and the pulmonary valve

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: doesn't open all the way.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: And right ventricular hypertrophy means the wall of the right ventricle is thicker than

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: normal.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Tetralogyofollow is repaired with open heart surgery, either soon after birth or in

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: infancy.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Pricle procedures may include temporary and complete repairs.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Your child may not be ready for a complete repair right away.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: In this case, a temporary repair, such as a shunt or a stent, can be placed until a complete

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: repair can be done.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Both repairs increase blood flow to the lungs and improve oxygen levels in the blood delivered

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: to the body.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: During a shunt procedure, the surgeon will open the chest wall to place a tube called a

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: shunt between a branch of the aorta and the right pulmonary artery.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: The shunt will provide another path for blood to get to the lungs to pick up oxygen.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: During a stent procedure, a tube called a catheter will be inserted through a small incision

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: into a blood vessel in the leg.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: The cardiologist will use the catheter to place a device called a stent inside the narrowed

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: area of the pulmonary artery.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: This allows more blood to flow to the lungs.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: A complete repair is done when your child is strong enough for the procedure.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: During the procedure, the pulmonary stenosis defect may be repaired in the following ways.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: If the main pulmonary artery is too small, it will be widened with a patch.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: If the pulmonary valve is too small, it may be widened with a patch or replaced.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: If extra heart muscle is blocking blood flow below the pulmonary valve, the surgeon will

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: remove the muscle tissue.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: These repairs will improve the flow of oxygen poor blood from the right ventricle to the lungs.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: As a result, the blood will get enough oxygen for the body's needs.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: To fix the ventricular septal defect, the surgeon will cover the hole with a patch.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: The patch will prevent the mixing of oxygen poor blood with oxygen rich blood.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: As a result, only oxygen rich blood will flow out of the aorta to the body from the left

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: ventricle.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Over time, fixing the pulmonary stenosis and closing the ventricular septal defect will

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: also fix the right ventricular hypertrophy.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Since the right ventricle won't have to work as hard to pump blood, its wall will return

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: to normal thickness.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: If your child still has a shunt from the temporary repair, it will either be removed or

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: clipped shut at the end of this procedure.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: Similarly, if your child had a stent placed, it will also be removed.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
text_en: To find out more about treatment for tetralogy of follow, talk to your health care provider.

title: ANH19227 Understanding Leukemia

title: ANH19228 Treatment Options for Leukemia
text_en: You or someone you care about may have been diagnosed with leukemia.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: You or someone you care about may have been diagnosed with a blood cancer.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: This video will help you understand how it can be managed.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Blood cancer, also known as hematologic cancer, begins in blood-forming tissue such as bone marrow.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Bone marrow is the spongy tissue inside your bones that makes blood cells,

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: or blood cancer may start within the cells of the immune system.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Examples of blood cancer are leukemia, lymphoma, and multiple myeloma.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Treatments such as chemotherapy, radiation therapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy,

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: and stem cell transplant may control blood cancers depending on the type of cancer and other factors.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: It is important to have a care plan in place not only for receiving therapy and personal care, but also for follow-up care.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: This includes scheduling and going to follow-up appointments.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: In addition to checking your general health and well-being, your doctor will want to check for any signs of the cancer returning

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: and check for health problems that may have resulted from treatment.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: For example, you may have tests and imaging studies performed, including taking samples of your blood,

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: fluid around your spinal cord, or bone marrow to check for cancer as relevant for your situation.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Continue taking all your medications as prescribed.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Do not stop taking them without talking to your doctor.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: It is helpful to take notes for points to discuss that your next doctor's visit.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: This could include any side effects you may have noticed.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: If you've had treatments such as chemotherapy, radiation therapy, or a bone marrow transplant,

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: follow any instructions you were given.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: There are lifestyle changes that can improve your health and help you feel better.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: There are benefits to stopping smoking.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: This lowers the risk for cancer returning or spreading.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: It's generally accepted that eating a healthy diet can help you feel better and stronger,

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: both during and after treatment.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Physical activities like walking, riding a bicycle or swimming can help you feel better and less tired.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Talk to your doctor before starting an exercise routine.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Knowing you have cancer can be overwhelming.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: You may have worries about your condition and how sick you could get,

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: how it will affect you and your family, treatments and hospital stays, medical bills, and your job.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: There are ways to find support for you.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Remember that your doctor and healthcare team are there to answer any questions you have.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: The following sources of support can help you, social workers, faith leaders, counselors, and support groups.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Talk to your doctor if you have any questions about your treatment plan, medications, or lifestyle changes

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: to help you manage your blood cancer.

title: ANH20232 Understanding CKD Anemia

title: ANH20233 Treatment Options for CKD Anemia
text_en: You or someone you care about may have been diagnosed with Anemia as a result of chronic

title: ANH22254 Understanding Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: Cyclecell anemia is an inherited blood disease that affects your red blood cells.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: Cycle cell anemia is an inherited blood disease that affects your red blood cells.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: In this disease, an abnormal protein molecule called hemoglobin S changes your disc-shaped

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: flexible red blood cells into rigid, sickle-shaped cells.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: Cycle cells can block blood flow and can cause pain and tissue damage.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: And sickle cells don't last as long as normal red blood cells.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: As a result, your blood has less red blood cells than normal.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: This condition is called anemia.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: Treatment options for sickle cell anemia may include medicines or procedures to help

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: relieve symptoms, or in certain cases a hematopoid-extem cell transplant to potentially cure the disease.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: Antibiotics and vaccine shots for diseases can help prevent infections.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: Your healthcare provider may advise you to take pain relievers for severe or chronic pain.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: One of the most common medicines for long-term treatment of sickle cell anemia is called

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: hydroxyuria.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: It helps reduce pain by preventing damage to your red blood cells.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: It also reduces the risk of long-term complications.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: Other prescribed medicines that help prevent complications and improve long-term outcomes include

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: chrysanlysmab, buxelator, and elglutamine.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: If you have an episode of very severe pain, you may have a procedure called a blood transfusion.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: This replaces the sickle cells with healthy red blood cells.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: For young patients who have, or are at risk for complications from sickle-cell anemia, a

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: hematopoid-extem cell transplant may be advised.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: It can cure some people who have this disease.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: For the transplant, you may be given chemotherapy drugs, radiation therapy, or both.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: This will destroy the abnormal blood-forming stem cells in your bone marrow.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: Then, you will receive healthy stem cells from either the bone marrow or blood of a donor.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: The donor is often a family member whose tissue type closely matches yours.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: The healthy blood-forming stem cells from the donor will travel through your blood to

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: your bone marrow, where they will make normal red blood cells instead of sickle cells.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
text_en: To find out more about treatments for sickle-cell anemia, talk to your healthcare provider.

title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: As you get older, your aortic valve may develop deposits of calcium that cause your leaflets

text_en: In this condition, your leaflets may not open fully.

text_en: This condition, called calcific aortic valve stenosis, creates a narrowed opening that reduces

text_en: As a result, the wall of your left ventricle thickens because it has to work harder to

title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach

title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: At the beginning of your procedure, your doctor will make a small incision in your groin.

title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: After creating an opening in a blood vessel, called the femoral artery, your doctor will

title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: insert a flexible tube called an introducer sheath.

title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: Through the sheath, your doctor will insert a flexible guide wire into your femoral artery.

title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: The guide wire will be passed through your femoral artery all the way up to your aorta.

text_en: Then the guide wire will be pushed through the opening in your aortic valve and into your

title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: left ventricle.

text_en: Your doctor will use the guide wire to pass a flexible tube called a catheter through your

text_en: aortic valve.

text_en: After deflating the balloon, your doctor will remove the catheter.

text_en: The replacement valve consists of a tissue valve surrounded by a wire mesh called a stent.

text_en: After placement of the new aortic valve, your doctor will deflate the balloon.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: If you have a severe problem with the mitral valve in your heart, your doctor may recommend

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: a robotic mitral valve replacement if it can't be repaired.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Your mitral valve is one of the four valves in your heart.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: It lies between your left atrium or upper chamber and left ventricle or lower chamber.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Oxygen-rich blood coming from your lungs goes into the left atrium.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: When your left atrium contracts, blood flows through your open mitral valve and into your

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: left ventricle.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: When your left ventricle contracts, your mitral valve closes to prevent blood from flowing

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: back into your left atrium.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Your mitral valve has two leaflets, an anterior or front leaflet, and a posterior or back

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: leaflet.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: String-like tissue, called tendinous cords, attach both leaflets to the inside of your

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: left ventricle.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: The tendinous cords help prevent blood from flowing back into your atrium.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: You may have one or both of the two main problems that can happen to your mitral valve.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: One problem is stenosis, in which the opening in your mitral valve is two narrow.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: The narrow opening reduces the amount of blood flowing through the valve from your left

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: atrium to your left ventricle.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Your mental valve stenosis may be caused by rheumatic fever or heart-birth defects.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: The other problem your valve may have is regurgitation, in which your mitral valve doesn't

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: close completely.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Retraction allows blood to leak back into your left atrium when your left ventricle contracts.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Mitral valve regurgitation may be caused by mitral valve prolapse, in which the valve leaflets

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: and cords become weak and loose.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Rheumatic heart disease, which may result from rheumatic fever, heart-birth defects, or

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: heart enlargement, called dilation, which may result from a heart attack.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Both stenosis and regurgitation strain your heart because it has to pump harder to move

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: oxygen-rich blood to your body.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Before your procedure, an intravenous line will be started.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: You may be given antibiotics through the IV to decrease your chance of infection.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: You will be given general anesthesia to make you unconscious and pain-free during the

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: procedure.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: A breathing tube will be inserted through your mouth and down your throat to help you

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: breathe during the procedure.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Your surgeon will typically make four or five small incisions on the right side of your

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: chest.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: An assistant will insert a special camera called an endoscope and other tools attached

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: to the robotic arms through the incisions.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Unlike standard laparoscopic instruments, these tools can rotate 360 degrees and have more

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: flexibility than the human wrist.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Seated at a special console, your surgeon will operate the robotic arms and the camera

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: with joystick-like controls and foot pedals.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: A computer will translate the exact movements of your surgeon's hands into precise movements

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: of the surgical tools.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Your surgeon will have a magnified three-dimensional stereoscopic view inside your body through

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: a high-definition vision system.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: After the robotic arms are in place, you will be placed on a heart-long machine.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: This machine will take over the functions of your heart and lungs while your heart is

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: stopped for your operation.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Your surgeon will begin the procedure on your heart by making an incision in your left

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: atrium to get to your mitral valve.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Next, all of your mitral valve's attachments to your heart will be cut and the valve will

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: be removed.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Your damaged mitral valve may be replaced with an artificial mechanical valve.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: A mechanical mitral valve will last a lifetime.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: However, they require you to take blood thinner medication for the rest of your life.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Alternatively, your mitral valve may be replaced with a valve made from human or animal tissue.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: A tissue mitral valve may not last as long as a mechanical valve.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: However, the natural tissue does not require blood thinner medication.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Once your new valve is in place, your surgeon will restart the beating of your heart and

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: remove the attachments to the heart-long machine.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: At the end of your procedure, your skin incisions will be closed with stitches, staples, surgical

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: glue, or skin closure strips.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: After your procedure, your breathing tube will be removed and you will be taken to the

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: intensive care unit or ICU for monitoring.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: You will be given pain medication as needed.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: You may continue to receive fluids and medications through your IV.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: You will be given blood thinner medication if you have a mechanical valve.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
text_en: Most patients are released from the hospital about a week after the procedure.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: Atherosclerosis is a life-threatening disease that may have begun to develop during childhood.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: This condition is a process in which deposits of fatty material, called plaque, build

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: up inside the walls of arteries, reducing or completely blocking blood flow.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: In extreme cases, this disease can lead to a heart attack or a stroke.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: These factors for atherosclerosis include high blood pressure, high cholesterol, tobacco

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: smoke, diabetes, obesity, and physical inactivity.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: Although the exact causes of atherosclerosis are not clear, many scientists think it begins

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: with damage to the inner wall of an artery, called the endothelium.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: Over time, substances traveling in the blood, such as cholesterol, fats, and cellular waste

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: products accumulate inside the damaged area of the arterial wall.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: Chemical reactions occurring within the build-up of material cause cholesterol molecules to

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: oxidize.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: This initiates an inflammatory response in which the endothelial cells, at the damage site,

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: release chemicals that signal a call for help.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: In response, monocytes from the bloodstream travel to the damage site.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: Demulation from oxidized cholesterol converts the monocytes into macrophages.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: The macrophages eat and digest the cholesterol molecules.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: As a result of this process, the macrophages change into foam cells, which accumulate to

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: form plaque.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: As the plaque increases in size, the arterial wall thickens and hardens.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: At the same time, smooth muscle cells within the arterial wall begin to multiply.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: Most of the smooth muscle cells move to the surface of the plaque.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: These cells contribute to the formation of a firm, fibrous cap covering the plaque.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: Eventually, the passageway through the artery narrows, enough to reduce blood flow and

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: the amount of oxygen received by the organs it supplies.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: Over time, the cap may erode and break open, releasing plaque into the bloodstream.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: The plaque can flow downstream and contribute to the formation of the blood clot, which

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: can stop blood flow.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: As a result, limited blood supply is available to the areas surrounding the partially blocked

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: artery, degrading and potentially killing the neighboring tissue.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: Significant damage in organs, such as the heart or brain, can result in a heart attack

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
text_en: or a stroke.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: Your doctor will insert a flexible guide wire into the artery and advance a balloon-tipped

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: catheter over this wire and into position at the side of the blockage.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: Rapid inflation and deflation of the small balloon pushes the vessel wall out,

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: reestablishing blood flow through the artery.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: You may feel some chest pain while this is happening.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: The same procedure is repeated for each blockage being treated.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: Afterwards, your doctor will withdraw the deflated balloon and catheter from your arteries.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: Some patients will receive a coronary stent, which is a small tube made of wire mesh,

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: designed to prop open the artery once it has been treated.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: A collapsed stent is placed over the deflated balloon as it's moved into position.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: When the balloon is inflated, the stent expands and locks into place.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: After the balloon and catheter are withdrawn, the stent remains behind to hold the artery open permanently.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: A coronary artery bypass graft, or cabbage, is performed to improve circulation to the

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: heart muscle in people with severe coronary artery disease.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: In this procedure, a healthy artery or vein from another part of the body is connected

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: or grafted to the blocked coronary artery.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: A grafted artery or vein bypasses the blocked portion of the coronary artery, carrying oxygen

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: rich blood to the heart muscle.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: One or more coronary arteries may be bypassed during a single operation.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: During a conventional bypass surgery, your surgeon begins by making an incision in the skin

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: over your breastbone or sternum.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: He or she will then cut the sternum and move your ribcage in order to get to your heart.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: Throughout the procedure, your circulatory system will be connected to a cardiopulmonary

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: bypass pump or heart lung machine.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: This machine temporarily performs the functions of your heart and lungs during the surgery,

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: allowing your heart to be stopped while the surgeon soars the grafts into place.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: One of two blood vessels is typically used for the grafts, the internal thoracic artery,

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: in the chest or the saffinous vein in the leg.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: For the internal thoracic artery graft, your surgeon will leave the upper end attached

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: to the subclavian artery and divert the lower end from your chest wall to your coronary

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: artery just beyond the blockage.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: Your surgeon will then sew the graft into place.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: For the saffinous vein graft, your surgeon will suture one end to the aorta and the

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: other end to the narrowed artery just beyond the blockage.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: With the grafts securely in place, your surgeon will use electrical signals to restore the

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: heart beat and attach a temporary pacemaker to the heart.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: Once your heart is again beating normally, the heart lung machine will be disconnected.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: Your surgeon will wire the breastbone back together and suture the skin incision closed.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
text_en: A temporary drainage tube will be placed through the skin beneath the incision.

title: ANS00254 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed
text_en: Once your heart is again beating normally, the heart lung machine will be disconnected.

text_en: Your surgeon will wire the breastbone back together and suture the skin incision closed.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Atherosclerosis is a life-threatening disease that may have begun to develop during childhood.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: This condition is a process in which deposits of fatty material, called plaque, build

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: up inside the walls of arteries, reducing or completely blocking blood flow.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Although the exact causes of atherosclerosis are not clear, many scientists think it begins

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: with damage to the inner wall of an artery, called the endothelium, substances traveling

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: in the blood, such as cholesterol, fats, and cellular waste products accumulate inside

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: the damaged area of the arterial wall.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Chemical reactions occurring within the build up of material cause cholesterol molecules

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: to oxidize.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: This initiates an inflammatory response in which the endothelial cells, at the damage site,

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: release chemicals that signal a call for help.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: In response, monocytes from the bloodstream travel to the damaged site.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Stimulation from oxidized cholesterol converts the monocytes into macrophages.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: The macrophages eat and digest the cholesterol molecules.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: As a result of this process, the macrophages change into foam cells, which accumulate

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: to form plaque.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: As the plaque increases in size, the arterial wall thickens and hardens.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: At the same time, smooth muscle cells within the arterial wall begin to multiply.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Most of the smooth muscle cells move to the surface of the plaque.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: These cells contribute to the formation of a firm, fibrous cap covering the plaque.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Eventually, the passageway through the artery narrows, enough to reduce blood flow, and

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: the amount of oxygen received by the organs it supplies.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Over time, the cap may erode and break open, releasing plaque into the bloodstream.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: The plaque can flow downstream and contribute to the formation of a blood clot, which can

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: stop blood flow.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: As a result, limited blood supply is available to the area surrounding the partially blocked

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: artery, degrading, and potentially killing the neighboring tissue.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will perform an abdominal aortic aneurysm repair to prevent the rupture of a

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: bulging area of your aorta.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Open abdominal aortic aneurysm repair.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will begin by making an incision from the breastbone to below the navel.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: He or she will then locate the aneurysm and clamp the aorta just above and below it.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will then open the aneurysm and sew an artificial graft the same size and

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: shape as a healthy aorta into place.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will stitch the wall of the aneurysm closed over the newly placed graft.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: The graft will reinforce the walls of the aorta.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will close the abdominal incision with stitches.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: The total length of this procedure is usually 4 to 6 hours.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
text_en: The largest artery in the body is the aorta, which is attached to the heart and receives blood directly from the left ventricle or main pumping chamber.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
text_en: The aorta is divided into four parts, the ascending aorta, aortic arch, thoracic aorta, and the abdominal aorta.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
text_en: The abdominal aorta is the last and largest section of the aorta.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
text_en: It supplies blood to the lower regions of your body, including the organs and the abdomen and pelvis.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
text_en: The abdominal aorta ends around naval level, where it splits into the two iliac arteries that carry blood to the legs.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will perform an abdominal aortic aneurysm repair to prevent the rupture of a bulging

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: area of your aorta, endovascular aneurysm repair.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will begin by making a small incision in your groin over your femoral artery.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Then he or she will insert a guide wire into the artery and gently push it up toward

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: the site of your aneurysm.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: A catheter or hollow tube will be passed over the guide wire and pushed toward your aneurysm.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Using live x-ray pictures as a guide, your surgeon will pass an endovascular stent graft,

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: a compressed fabric and metal tube, through the catheter to the aneurysm.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Then he or she will open the graft which will be held in place with metallic hooks and stents.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon may insert additional graft components to extend the graft into the blood vessels supplying your legs.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Once the graft is in place, your blood will flow through it, not into the surrounding aneurysm sac,

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: which will remove the pressure from your aneurysm.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Finally, your surgeon will remove the catheters and close the incisions in your groin.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: The procedure will generally take 2-3 hours.

title: ANS00423 Arterial Stenosis
text_en: Fatty deposits called atherosclerotic plaques can build up inside your arteries, narrowing the passageway and interfering with blood flow.

title: ANS00423 Arterial Stenosis
text_en: This arterial blockage is called stenosis.

title: ANS00423 Arterial Stenosis
text_en: Insufficient blood flow to a tissue generally causes pain.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Non-cardiac percutaneous transliminal angioplasty.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: A surgeon will perform a non-cardiac percutaneous transliminal angioplasty, or NC-PTA, to restore blood flow

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: in an artery block by an atherosclerotic plaque.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: To begin the procedure, you'll be placed on a table equipped with a fluoroscope.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: A fluoroscope is capable of taking x-ray images of many parts of the body from multiple angles.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Your doctor will numb your skin around where the catheter is to be inserted.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Since the arteries of the leg are most easily accessed, the femoral artery in the groin serves as the insertion site in most cases.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Your doctor will initially gain access to the artery by inserting a hollow needle through which the balloon-tipped catheter is passed.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Watching on the fluoroscopy screen, your doctor will gently advance the catheter until it is positioned in the artery being treated and eject a special die.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: This die allows the fluoroscope to take x-ray images called angiograms, which lets your doctor identify the exact location of the blockages.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Next, your doctor will move the catheter until the deflated balloon at its tip is positioned at the site of the stenosis.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Then, by rapidly inflating and deflating the balloon, your doctor will gently push the plaque and vessel wall out, partially re-establishing blood flow.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: This procedure may be repeated for any additional blockages.

title: ANS00425 The Heart
text_en: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood through the body.

title: ANS00425 The Heart
text_en: It consists of four chambers.

title: ANS00425 The Heart
text_en: The upper chambers are the right and left atria.

title: ANS00425 The Heart
text_en: The lower chambers are the right and left ventricles.

title: ANS00425 The Heart
text_en: The right and left atria connect to their respective ventricles, the right ventricle connects

title: ANS00425 The Heart
text_en: to the pulmonary artery, and the left ventricle to the aorta.

title: ANS00425 The Heart
text_en: Those are located at these connections to ensure that blood moves in only one direction.

title: ANS00426 The Aortic Valve
text_en: The aortic valve is located between the left ventricle and the aorta.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: And aortic valve replacement is performed to restore the valve to normal function.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Before your procedure, an IV line will be started in your arm to provide fluids and medications.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: A catheter will be inserted into your bladder to drain urine.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Aortic valve replacements are done under general anesthesia.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: This means you will be asleep for the duration of the operation.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Add a tube will be placed through your mouth and into your windpipe to help you breathe

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: during surgery.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Once the anesthesia takes effect, your surgeon will begin by making an incision in the chest.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: The traditional incision is made in the middle of the chest, overlying the breastbone or sternum.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: The surgeon then separates the sternum to expose the pericardium, which is a protective

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: membrane encircling the heart.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Your surgeon will then open the pericardium to expose the heart.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: At this point, your heart will be connected to a heart lung machine.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Tubes are used to reroute your blood into this machine, which takes over the functions

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: of the heart and lungs during the operation.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Once the heart lung machine has taken over, your heart will be temporarily stopped and

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: the blood inside removed.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: This keeps the aortic valve still and allows your surgeon to see it.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Your surgeon will open the aorta to expose the aortic valve, make an incision around the

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: edge of the damaged valve, and remove it.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Your surgeon will then carefully sew the replacement valve into place and close the aorta

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: with stitches.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: At this point, blood will again be allowed to flow into your heart to check the function

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: of the new valve.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: If needed, an electric shock will be given to restart the heart.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Once your heart is beating on its own, it will be detached from the heart lung machine.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Generally, temporary pacemaker wires will be placed on the surface of the heart to ensure

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: the heart maintains a normal rhythm during recovery.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: After surgery, you'll be taken to the intensive care unit, where your vital functions will

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: be monitored.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: You will still have a breathing tube in your throat, which will prevent you from talking.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Several tubes will be in place to drain blood and fluid from your incision, and your bladder

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: catheter will still be draining your urine.

title: ANS00431 Carotid Arteries
text_en: In your neck are two major blood vessels called the carotid arteries, which carry oxygen-rich

title: ANS00431 Carotid Arteries
text_en: blood from your heart to your brain.

title: ANS00431 Carotid Arteries
text_en: You can feel the pulse of the carotid artery in the side of your neck.

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
text_en: In your neck are two major blood vessels called the carotid arteries, which carry oxygen-rich

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
text_en: blood from your heart to your brain.

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
text_en: You can feel the pulse of the carotid artery in the side of your neck.

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
text_en: Fatty deposits called atherosclerotic plaques can build up inside the carotid artery.

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
text_en: These plaques can narrow the passageway of the artery and interfere with blood flow

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
text_en: to the brain.

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
text_en: This blockage or narrowing is called a stenosis.

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
text_en: If blood flow to the brain is blocked temporarily, a transient ischemic attack, also known as a

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
text_en: TIA, may occur.

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
text_en: When the blood flow is completely obstructed, as in a carotid artery blockage, a stroke

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
text_en: can occur.

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: If you are a mobile for an extended period of time because of surgery, pregnancy, illness,

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: or travel, you may be at risk for deep vein thrombosis or DVT.

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: A condition where a blood clot forms in a deep vein in the body, typically the leg.

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Unlike arteries which rely on the heart to force blood through them, veins transport

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: blood with the help of muscle contractions while you move and walk.

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Things also have valves that keep blood from flowing backward and pooling in your arms

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: and legs.

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: When you are a mobile for an extended period, your venous circulation slows down.

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: As a result, clotting factors in the blood may cause the slow moving blood to clump together,

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: forming a blood clot or thrombus.

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: This condition is called deep vein thrombosis or DVT.

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Common symptoms of DVT include swelling of the leg or a vein in the leg, pain or tenderness

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: in the area, increased warmth in the skin, and red or discolored skin.

title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
text_en: In some DVT cases, a blood clot may detach from a deep vein and join the bloodstream.

title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
text_en: The clot travels from the deep veins of the leg through the veins of the abdomen,

title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
text_en: into the heart, and finally lodges in the pulmonary artery or its branches, blocking blood flow

title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
text_en: to the lungs.

title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
text_en: This condition, called pulmonary embolism, is very serious and may permanently damage

title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
text_en: a portion of the lung.

title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
text_en: If a clot is large or if there are many clots, pulmonary embolism may also cause death.

title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
text_en: If you experience symptoms of PE, please tell your doctor or nurse immediately.

title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
text_en: These symptoms may include, shortness of breath, a sudden sharp pain in the chest, a feeling

title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
text_en: of lightheadedness or dizziness, rapid or irregular heartbeat, or coughing that produces

title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
text_en: blood.

title: ANS11476 Atrial Fibrillation (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Atrial fibrillation is a type of arrhythmia, which is an abnormality in the pace or force

title: ANS11476 Atrial Fibrillation (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: of your heartbeat.

title: ANS11476 Atrial Fibrillation (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: If you have atrial fibrillation, the heart's upper chambers contract in a fast, twitchy

title: ANS11476 Atrial Fibrillation (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: fashion, resulting in an irregular, uncoordinated heartbeat.

title: ANS11477 Four Heart Chambers
text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ made up of four chambers, the right and left atria, and the right and left ventricles.

title: ANS11477 Four Heart Chambers
text_en: During a normal heartbeat, the chambers of your heart contract and relax in a rhythmic pattern to deliver a consistent flow of blood throughout your body.

title: ANS11477 Four Heart Chambers
text_en: The right atrium collects blood from the body, and the right ventricle pumps it to the lungs, which bring oxygen to and remove carbon dioxide from the blood.

title: ANS11477 Four Heart Chambers
text_en: At the same time, the left atrium receives blood from the lungs, and the left ventricle pumps it out to supply your body's tissues with oxygen and nutrients.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
text_en: An echocardiogram is a common test using sound waves to map out the shape and size of your heart,

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
text_en: thus allowing your doctor to see how well your heart pumps blood and look for abnormalities of the heart valves and chambers.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
text_en: In a trans thoracic echocardiogram, your synographer will put a cool gel on your chest.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
text_en: Your synographer will move a transducer firmly through the gel, sending painless sound waves to your heart.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
text_en: The sound waves bounce off your heart and echo back to the transducer.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
text_en: The sound waves are converted to moving images of your heart muscles, chambers, and valves on a video screen.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
text_en: A Doppler echocardiogram records echoes from blood cells.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
text_en: The speed and direction of the blood flow indicate how well blood gets around inside your heart.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
text_en: A stress echocardiogram evaluates images of the heart before and after exercise or following medication that simulates exercise on the heart.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
text_en: Comparing your echo results before and after activity provides your doctor information about certain heart problems, such as coronary artery disease, that may only occur with exertion.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
text_en: A transisophageal echocardiogram sends sound waves through a transducer past gently down your throat while your under sedation.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
text_en: This test provides a closer, more detailed evaluation of your heart.

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: Your heart pumps oxygenated blood throughout your body.

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: Rhythmic electrical impulses inside your heart cause its muscular walls to contract, ensuring

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: your heart beats steadily.

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: If problems occur with your heart's electrical system, you may develop an abnormal, potentially

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: life-threatening heart beat called arrhythmia.

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: Your doctor may want you to have an implantable cardio-verter defibrillator or ICD to restore

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: the heart's normal rhythm and prevent sudden cardiac death.

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: During the procedure, your doctor will thread a small wire through a vein until it reaches

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: your right ventricle.

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: The wires continuously monitor the heart's rhythm.

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: After verifying the wire is in the correct location and works properly, your doctor will

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: attach the wire to the ICD generator.

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: Then your doctor will insert the ICD device under the skin of your chest below the collarbone.

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: The battery-powered ICD constantly monitors your heart beat using a computer.

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: If a life-threatening arrhythmia occurs, the ICD delivers an electrical shock to your

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: heart, resetting your heart beat back to normal.

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
text_en: The ICD can also be programmed to paste the heart and restore its natural rhythm.

title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
text_en: Any condition or disease that damages your heart's ability to pump blood can cause

title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
text_en: heart failure.

title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
text_en: For example, the most common cause is coronary artery disease in which blood vessels that

title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
text_en: supply your heart muscle, called coronary arteries, become blocked by a fatty substance called

title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
text_en: plaque.

title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
text_en: Other common causes of heart failure include uncontrolled high blood pressure and high

title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
text_en: blood sugar in diabetes.

title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
text_en: Less common causes of heart failure include diseased, infected or damaged heart valves,

title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
text_en: your regular heartbeats, called arrhythmias, heart defects, poisons or substance abuse, lung

title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
text_en: diseases, and breathing problems during sleep, called sleep apnea.

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: If you have heart failure, your heart has lost the ability to pump enough blood to meet

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: your body's needs.

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: You may have weak or damaged ventricular walls that are not able to push enough blood

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: out of your heart.

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: You may also have stiff and thickened ventricular walls that do not allow your heart to fill

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: with enough blood.

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: If you have left-sided heart failure, your left ventricle does not deliver enough oxygen

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: rich blood to your body, making you feel tired and out of breath.

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: Your failing left ventricle also increases the blood pressure in the blood vessels between

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: your lungs and left ventricle.

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: This increased pressure forces fluid out of your blood and into your lung tissues, which

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: makes it difficult for you to breathe.

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: If you have right-sided heart failure, your right ventricle is unable to contract with

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: enough force to push blood to your lungs.

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: The result is a build-up of blood in your veins, which causes a build-up of fluid called

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: edema throughout the tissues in your body.

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: Over time, heart failure on either side of your heart results in weakened and large

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: ventricles that deliver less blood to your body.

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: To make up for the decreased amount of blood, your nervous system releases stress hormones

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: that increase the speed and force of your heart beat.

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: Unfortunately, the continued release of these stress hormones makes your heart fail

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
text_en: your worse because they damage the muscle cells in your ventricles.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: This video will help you understand some lifestyle changes you can make to treat heart failure.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: Please watch the entire video to learn about lifestyle changes for heart failure.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: As a heart failure patient, you may need to make some lifestyle changes to prevent further damage to your heart.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: If you smoke, quitting is one of the most important things you can do for your heart.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: Work with your healthcare provider to create a nutritious eating plan that also helps you maintain a healthy weight.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: A heart healthy diet includes vegetables, fruits, lean meats and whole grains, as well as low fat or fat-free dairy products.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: High calorie foods such as cookies and soda should be replaced with fruits and vegetables that don't contain added salt and sugar.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: Examples of foods in a healthy diet include vegetables such as carrots, broccoli and tomatoes, fruits such as apples, oranges and strawberries,

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: lean proteins such as fish, chicken and beans,

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: dairy foods such as low fat yogurt and cheese,

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: starches such as whole wheat bread and sweet potatoes,

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: and healthy drinks such as water and fat-free milk.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: Reduce the amount of salt and sugar you eat.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: Keep sodium levels below 2000mg to reduce the amount of water retained by your body.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: Substitute solid fats with heart healthy oils such as olive oil.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: Limit the amount of fluid you drink.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: Ask your healthcare provider how much fluid you should drink each day.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: And remember, a heart healthy diet doesn't include alcohol.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: Also, check with your healthcare provider about an exercise plan that's right for you.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: 30 minutes of daily low impact exercise can help strengthen your heart.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
text_en: Talk to your healthcare provider if you have any questions about lifestyle changes to help you manage heart failure.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
text_en: Your doctor may prescribe one or more types of medication to treat your heart failure.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
text_en: Diuretic medications reduce the swelling in your body by increasing the amount of urine produced by your kidneys.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
text_en: Ace inhibitors are medications that allow your blood vessels to expand.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
text_en: This helps decrease your blood pressure and prevents further damage to your heart by making it easier for your heart to pump blood.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
text_en: Beta blockers are medications that block the effects of stress hormones on your heart.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
text_en: Although beta blockers slow down your heartbeat, they are mainly used to protect your heart muscle from the long-term damage caused by stress hormones.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
text_en: This video will help you understand the main surgical procedures done to treat heart failure.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
text_en: Please watch the entire video to learn about the surgeries for heart failure.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
text_en: Surgery for advanced heart failure include coronary artery bypass surgery to improve blood flow to your heart muscle.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
text_en: And heart valve reconstruction surgery to improve blood flow through your heart.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
text_en: If the heart remains weak with standard heart failure treatments,

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
text_en: advanced treatment options for severe heart failure include insertion of a pump for your heart.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
text_en: A ventricular assist device, or VAD, is a mechanical pump that's used to support heart function and blood flow in people who have weakened hearts.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
text_en: The device takes blood from a lower chamber of the heart and helps pump it to the body and vital organs just as a healthy heart would.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
text_en: Another advanced treatment for severe heart failure is a heart transplant.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
text_en: If you have advanced heart failure, your surgeon will determine the best type of treatment for you.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
text_en: The first step is to get the heart transplant.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
text_en: The second step is to get the heart transplant.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
text_en: The second step is to get the heart transplant.

title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients your

file: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart.mp4
title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
text_en: body needs.

file: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart.mp4
title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
text_en: The pumping sections of the heart are the right atrium, right ventricle, left atrium,

file: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart.mp4
title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
text_en: and left ventricle.

file: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart.mp4
title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
text_en: In your heart, oxygen poor blood flows from your body through large veins into your

file: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart.mp4
title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
text_en: right atrium.

file: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart.mp4
title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
text_en: Next, your blood moves into your right ventricle, which contracts, sending blood out of the

file: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart.mp4
title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
text_en: heart to pick up oxygen from your lungs.

file: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart.mp4
title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
text_en: Oxygen rich blood moves out of your lungs into your left atrium, then moves into your

file: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart.mp4
title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
text_en: left ventricle.

file: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart.mp4
title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
text_en: Finally, your left ventricle contracts, sending oxygen rich blood out of your heart to

file: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart.mp4
title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
text_en: your body.
